Guide to Using A Spreadsheet This document is a simple guide to using spreadsheets. We outline basic spreadsheet design, entering data, creating graphs, and converting your graphs to image files that can be uploaded as images on your research webpage. We use Microsoft Excel in our examples as it is the most common spreadsheet application and appears on most PCs. A spreadsheet is basically a grid of cells. It is a powerful scientific tool for storing information. When you open a spreadsheet, you'll find a page with numbers running down the left side of the page (1,2,3,4, ) and letters running across the top of the page (A, B, C, D, ). The letters act as COLUMN headings (cells running down the page). The numbers act as ROW headings (cells running across the page). Scientists use the column and row headings to identify each cell. For example: the very first cell on a spreadsheet is A1. The next cell in the same row is B1, the third C1, the fourth D1 and so on. The second cell in the A1 column is A2, the third A3, the fourth A4 and so on. You now know how to find and identify any cell on a spreadsheet! Set up and label your spreadsheet so others can easily follow the information. Carefully think about the best way to show the information you will record. Ask yourself if the labels and data show what research question you are asking. Can you see this easily in the way you have set up your spreadsheet? IMPORTANT! In setting up your spreadsheet, be very clear about what you are trying to measure and make it simple for others to understand. There are different ways to set up your data on a spreadsheet. Over time, you will learn various means to display the data you are collecting. Let's use a simple example in which we are measuring the growth 5 seed types under identical conditions. The research question is: "Do clover, wheat, rye, corn and soybean seeds grow at the same rate? Every day they measured the sprouting seeds. Time (in days) is their independent variable. Length of the seeds is their dependent variable. The student researchers collected data on 10 seeds of each species and calculated an average growth rate (growth in centimeters per day) for each species. Figure 1 is the spreadsheet they created. - In cell B1 the team gave their spreadsheet a title called "Table 1: Sprout Growth Rate for Various Seed Types." This helps people understand how you are testing your research question. - In cell C2 and (row 3), they show they are recording measurements for 14 days. - Time (in days) Row 3 will become the x-axis of the graph they make in the next step. - In Column B they label rows with each species in their study. They labeled rows B4-B8. They recorded the average length each species over the 14 days of the study. Tip: To enter data in a cell, click on the box associated with the day and seed type, and type in your measurement for each day beginning with day 1 in box C4 and so on down the column or across the row. /li> - The average length measured for each species will become the y-axis for the graph they make in the next step - Once all your data is entered on the spreadsheet, highlight the area your headings and data occupy by clicking on cell B3 and dragging across all of the boxes you to P8 (all your data and the x & y axis cells should be outlined and appear as a light highlighted color). - Now click the graph icon button in the menu at the top of the screen. - Select the type of graph you would like to create from the Chart Type menu. In our example, we will use a Line graph. - Then select the second option down on the left, "Line with markers displayed at each data value", from the Chart Sub-Type menu. Double click it. A preview of your graph will appear. - Click on the Data Range tab at the top of the preview graph, and make sure the Series button is set for Rows. - Click NEXT. - Now it will ask you to fill in your Title (use your Table heading from cell B1), your x-axis (enter Days) and your y-axis (enter Length in CM). - Click NEXT - On the Place Chart box, make sure the "As object in" button is checked and your spreadsheet is in the box. - Click the Finish button. A standard copy of your graph will appear. We'll let you play with the program to figure out how to modify the looks! It should be something like Figure 2 below. If you look at EastCentralU7 - (click here) , they have added graphs from their data sheet to enhance their research webpage. What they did was to convert an Excel sheet graphic into an image file (.jpg works well). Try the following steps yourself. 1. Open your Excel document containing the graph/chart you want to turn into an image. 2. Open the Paint program in your application folder (or another image program, we chose Paint as it comes with most PCs). 3. Go back to your Excel document and click on the graph you have created. Go to the Edit menu and select Copy. 4. Go back to the Paint program, select File and click on New. When it opens the box, hit Paste. 5. Now go back up to the File menu and select Save As and then select .jpg as the Save as type at the bottom of the box and click Save (make sure your remember where you save it). 6. Login to PlantingScience with your username and password. 7. Go to your research page, click the Edit this link. 8. Scroll down to the Images section on your edit page. 9. Click Browse next to Supporting Image #1 and find your image on your computer. 10. Click on the image and click Open or Upload. 11. Scroll down to Actions at the very bottom of your edit page. Make sure Update is showing in the box and click Go. Your image should now appear on your research page. You can add up to six images using the six Supporting Image boxes. For more information, see the Student Web Guide. Questions? [circle of silver men goes only part way around the silver circle on the black base] Periodic events are those that happen regularly in a repetitive way. The name periodic comes from period, which is the length of time required for a wave to complete one cycle. Periodic phenomena include: Listen, I don't want you to play with it. [grunts] I wish you would let me do this, instead of crawling over every time. Would a telephone book for each island? Return to Solutions Menu The graphic just below shows a U-shaped tube. Notice that dividing line with the arrow pointing at it. What that line represents is REAL important. The two sides of the tube (A and B) have the same volume of solution at the start. For this example, side A has a 1.0-M sucrose solution, while side B is pure water. The line with an arrow pointed at it represents a semipermeable membrane. This is the item that makes the osmosis happen. A semipermeable membrane allows solvent molecules to pass through it, BUT NOT SOLUTE! That's important. Solvent can go either direction easily, but solute is stuck on the side it's on. It cannot move past the semipermeable membrane. This is because the holes in the membrane are too small for the molecules to get through. By the way, sometimes molecules will be discussed and sometimes ions. IT DOES NOT MATTER what is in solution. Only how many items are dissolved, be they molecules or ions. OK, back to the tube. Side A has a greater concentration of SOLUTE. That should be pretty easy, since poor side B doesn't have any solute at all. The key here is that the semipermeable membrane locks the solute in place. It is blocked from movement by the membrane. So now lets look at the SOLVENT, which is water. (Keep in mind that the water can move across the membrane.) Which side has a greater concentration of water? The answer is side B. In side A, some water molecules have been replaced by sugar molecules, so there are less water molecules in the same volume as side B. THIS IS REAL IMPORTANT! The side that is LESS concentrated will have MORE water. If the explanation is getting too long, then take a break for a moment. If you're at home, go get a drink or clean house for five minutes. Then help your dad revive your mom! If you're at school, take a big stretch and rub yor eyes. Then, back to work. OK, next point. There is a well-supported idea in science that things tend to move from areas of high concentration to low concentration. We'll leave off proving it, because, well frankly, you're not knowledgable enough yet. (The fact that the ChemTeam isn't either is beside the point!) Well, in our example what can move? The solute can't because it's blocked by the semipermeable membrane. However, the solvent can move and it does. There is a net movement of solvent from side B across to side A. The volume of side B will go down and the volume of side A will go up. That means the sucrose concentration will decrease from 1.0-M. Here is the graphic showing that: For this next example, side A has a 1.0-M sucrose solution, while side B is 2.0-M sucrose. Remember the sides start out equal in volume. The side with more solute is the side with LESS solvent. Water will move toward the side with the greater amount of solute. The result after some time is this: The right-hand side will eventually stop moving up (and the left-hand side down). This is because the right-hand side's column is exerting pressure. The column rise can also be made to stop by the application of mechanical pressure on the surface of the solution. This is discussed in the Reverse Osmosis file. The amount of osmotic pressure exerted can be measured and explained via an easy equation. This is discussed in the Osmosis Equation file. [Ross saw that all the popcorn that was left for Santa was gone.] = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Mawhinney/060027c.cha = = = [Sic pushes the toy car; it rolls off the table] Title: How to Allow Advertisers to Track You in Safari on an iPhone Introduction: While many people prefer to limit their online tracking for privacy reasons, there might be situations where you want advertisers to track your activity in Safari on your iPhone. This could be useful if you're interested in personalized ads or if you're participating in market research. Here is a comprehensive guide on enabling cross-site tracking in Safari on your iOS device. Step 1: Open Settings App Find and tap on the grey gear icon labeled 'Settings.' The settings app allows you to customize various aspects of your iPhone, including Safari preferences. Step 2: Scroll Down and Tap Safari In the main menu of the Settings app, scroll down until you see 'Safari,' which should be located between 'Contacts' and 'Sounds & Haptics.' Tapping this option will take you to the Safari configuration page. Step 3: Locate Privacy & Security Section Once on the Safari configuration screen, look for the section titled 'Privacy & Security.' It contains several options related to user data protection and management while browsing with Safari. Step 4: Disable Prevent Cross-Site Tracking Option Under the 'Privacy & Security' heading, find the switch that says 'Prevent Cross-Site Tracking.' By default, this feature should be enabled (switch toggled right), displaying green color. To allow advertisers to track you across websites, toggle the switch left so that it turns gray, disabling this setting. Key Tips and Guidelines for Step 4: * Understanding what 'Prevent Cross-Site Tracking' does: Cookies are small text files stored by web browsers containing information about users and their interactions with specific sites. When disabled, this feature stops third-party cookies from sharing collected cookie data among different domains, thereby preventing cross-site tracking. Enabling it means you permit advertisers to monitor your behavior as you navigate through multiple websites, offering them valuable insights into consumer habits. * Consider potential consequences: Allowing cross-site tracking may lead to more targeted advertisements but can also compromise user privacy. Carefully weigh these factors before proceeding. Step 5: Confirm Changes Saved Exit out of the Settings app and open up Safari. Visit any website to ensure that the changes have been applied correctly. If the site prompts you to accept cookies, do so; otherwise, verify whether your activities are being tracked via other methods such as browser fingerprinting techniques. Conclusion: By following these steps, you have successfully configured Safari on your iPhone to let advertisers track your internet usage across various websites. Remember always to consider both advantages and disadvantages when making decisions concerning digital privacy and security. Title: Get People to Believe You Have Ice Powers Introduction: This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to convince people that you possess ice powers. It's important to note that this is meant for entertainment purposes only and should not be used to deceive or manipulate others. Always respect their beliefs and feelings. Step 1: Understand Your Audience Before attempting to make someone believe in your supposed ice powers, understand who they are, what they know about such matters, and how open-minded they might be. This will help tailor your approach accordingly. For example, convincing a group of children may require simpler demonstrations than trying to persuade adults. Key Tip: Remember, it's easier to convince those who already have an interest or belief in supernatural phenomena. Step 2: Research Real Illusions Look into existing magic tricks or illusions related to creating ice or cold sensations. Many professional magicians perform these types of acts, so learning from them can give you credible ideas. Websites like YouTube offer numerous videos showcasing various techniques. Guideline: Never claim these are actual 'powers'; instead, refer to them as 'tricks', maintaining honesty while still entertaining. Step 3: Develop a Backstory Creating a compelling backstory adds authenticity to your character. Explain how you discovered your abilities, any challenges faced, and rules surrounding your power (e.g., only works when touching water). Keep it consistent but vague enough to allow flexibility. Key Tip: Make sure your story aligns with common tropes found in popular culture regarding individuals with special abilities. Step 4: Practice Your Delivery The way you present yourself significantly impacts whether people take you seriously. Speak confidently, maintain eye contact, and control body language to exude confidence. If you seem unsure or nervous, others could doubt your claims more easily. Guideline: Consider practicing in front of a mirror or recording yourself to identify areas needing improvement. Step 5: Perform Simple Tricks First Start by performing simple tricks that create the illusion of having ice powers. These could include producing 'frosted' glassware, making small objects feel extremely cold, or causing steam to rise dramatically when pouring hot liquid onto a surface you previously touched. Key Tip: Gradually build up complexity as trust grows; starting too strong might raise suspicion rather than curiosity. Step 6: Utilize Props Carefully Using props wisely enhances your performance. However, being caught using them could harm your credibility. Therefore, ensure they're hidden well, introduced subtly, and disposed of quickly after usage. Guideline: Opt for everyday items disguised as props, reducing chances of discovery. Step 7: Manage Expectations Don't promise extraordinary feats beyond your capabilities. Instead, underpromise and overdeliver, leaving room for surprise but minimizing disappointment if something doesn't go as planned. Key Tip: Be prepared for skepticism and questions; respond calmly and honestly without becoming defensive or aggressive. Step 8: Maintain Mystery Keeping some aspects unexplained fuels intrigue. Refrain from revealing every detail; letting people wonder adds to the allure of your persona. Guideline: Balance between sharing just enough information to keep them interested and holding back to preserve mystery. Conclusion: Remember, regardless of how convincing your act may be, never mislead others into believing you truly possess supernatural abilities. The goal here is to entertain and spark imagination, not deception. With the beans and then they'll be like meat. Title: How to Hold a Mehndi Cone Mehndi, also known as henna, is a traditional art form that involves applying intricate designs on the skin using a cone filled with paste made from crushed henna leaves. The cone is held delicately while drawing patterns, requiring practice and skill. This tutorial will guide you through the process of properly holding a mehndi cone to help you create beautiful and consistent designs. **Step 1:** Prepare Your Mehndi Cone Before learning how to hold a mehndi cone, make sure your cone has been prepared correctly. Purchase ready-made cones or prepare them yourself by rolling thin plastic sheets into a cone shape, securing one end with glue, and filling it with pre-made or homemade mehndi paste. Make certain the opening at the tip is small enough (about 2 mm) to allow for controlled flow during application. **Step 2:** Choose the Right Hand Position Decide whether you are right-handed or left-handed; this determines which hand will be used to hold the cone. Most people find it more comfortable to draw mehndi designs with their dominant hand. However, if you're ambidextrous, choose whichever feels most natural. **Step 3:** Grip the Upper Portion of the Cone Gently pinch the top portion of the cone between your thumb and index finger. Apply light pressure so that you can feel the texture of the material without causing any deformation. Be careful not to squeeze too hard, as this could alter the size of the tip opening and affect the consistency of your design. **Step 4:** Support the Lower Part of the Cone Place your middle finger under the lower part of the cone for added stability. Rest the bottom edge of the cone along your middle finger knuckle. Keep your ring finger and pinky fingers relaxed and slightly apart from the cone, providing additional support when needed. **Key Tip:** Maintain a Light Grip Always maintain a light grip on the cone, ensuring its structure remains intact but still flexible enough for precise movements. A loose yet firm hold enables better control over line thickness and curvature, ultimately resulting in neater and more intricate designs. **Step 5:** Adjust Posture and Body Position Sit upright with good posture, keeping your back straight and shoulders relaxed. Place both feet flat on the ground and position the paper or surface you'll be working on directly in front of you. This ergonomic setup reduces strain on your hands and allows for smoother motions while drawing. **Guideline:** Practice Consistent Pressure As you begin drawing, pay attention to the amount of pressure applied to the cone. Too much force may cause excess paste to ooze out uncontrollably, whereas insufficient pressure might result in weak lines and inconsistencies within your pattern. Gradually increase or decrease pressure according to specific design requirements. **Step 6:** Test Flow Regularly Periodically check the flow rate of the paste coming out of the cone tip. If necessary, adjust your grip to widen or narrow the tip opening accordingly. Ensuring proper flow ensures even distribution of paste throughout your design, leading to visually pleasing results. By following these steps and practicing regularly, you'll develop proficiency in holding a mehndi cone effectively, enabling you to produce stunning and professional-looking designs with ease. Happy Henna-ing! Do you want to see some other toys? Should we close up the store for today? And then it won't be as good. Title: A Fun Game with Coin Tosses! Hey kids, have you ever thought about playing a game involving coin tosses? It's not just heads or tails guessing; instead, we get to create our own sequences using two coin tosses! This means we choose either "heads-heads" (HH), "heads-tails" (HT), "tails-heads" (TH), or "tails-tails" (TT) combinations. Let's find out more about this exciting game! Imagine there are two players - Player A and Player B. Both will select their unique combination without knowing what the other has chosen. After selecting, a coin will be tossed again and again until one of the selected combinations shows up. Once that happens, the lucky player whose turn it is when the combination appears will win! Sounds fun, doesn't it? Now here comes the interesting part - imagine if the coin was slightly unfair. In our game, getting 'heads' results in a 2/3 chance while getting 'tails' occurs only 1/3 of the time. As a smart grade-schooler, should you like to go first or second? Would choosing between HH, HT, TH, or TT make any difference? Let me share some insights on why picking your order matters. Suppose you decide to take turns showing off your magic tricks by revealing your chosen combinations one after another. You could start by showing yours, followed by your friend's reveal. Alternatively, you may let them show their trick first before unveiling yours. Which way do you think increases your chances of winning? Well, it turns out that choosing your sequence last gives you better odds! But don't worry too much about complicated math yet. Instead, consider thinking through strategies based on common sense. For example, since the coin favors landing on 'heads', wouldn't it be wise to include at least one head in your combo, so you have higher chances of seeing it sooner during the repetitive tosses? Try experimenting with friends and family members and discover which strategy works best for you! Remember, even though understanding probabilities and statistics requires deeper knowledge beyond grade school, experiencing these concepts early on helps build curiosity towards learning them later. So keep exploring, asking questions, and enjoying games that challenge your mind! Happy tossing! Adding a boyfriend on MovieStarPlanet (MSP) is an exciting way to enhance your gameplay experience by engaging with other players and creating a virtual relationship. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step, ensuring clarity and practicality so that you can easily follow and apply these instructions. Step 1: Create Your Movie Star Avatar If you haven't already done so, create your own unique movie star avatar. Choose from various customization options such as skin tone, hair color, eye shape, clothing, and accessories to represent yourself within the MSP world. Having a well-designed avatar makes you more approachable and attractive to potential partners. Key Tip: Make sure your avatar reflects your personality and interests, making it easier for like-minded individuals to connect with you. Step 2: Enter the Moviestar Planet World Log into your account and enter the main hub area where you can interact with other players, watch movies, chat, and participate in activities. Familiarize yourself with the user interface and take note of different locations where you might find potential partners. Step 3: Search for Potential Boyfriends To search for potential boyfriends, visit popular areas within the game where users tend to congregate, such as the ArtBook or Cinema. Look for players who share similar interests, hobbies, or appearance preferences. Pay attention to their profile information and artwork to get a better understanding of their personality. Guideline: Be respectful and considerate when approaching new people; avoid spamming messages or being overly aggressive in your pursuit of a romantic partner. Step 4: Initiate Contact Once you have identified someone you would like to pursue a relationship with, initiate contact by sending them a friendly message. Start off by introducing yourself and mention something specific about their profile or artwork that caught your interest. Avoid using generic pickup lines or coming across too strong initially. Key Tip: Building a genuine connection based on shared interests increases the likelihood of forming a meaningful relationship. Step 5: Engage in Conversation After breaking the ice, continue the conversation by asking open-ended questions related to their hobbies, favorite movies, or personal experiences. Show sincere interest in getting to know them better while sharing details about yourself as well. Remember, communication forms the foundation of any successful relationship. Step 6: Exchange Usernames If both parties are comfortable with taking things further, exchange usernames to establish direct contact outside of public chat rooms. This allows for more private conversations and strengthens your bond. Step 7: Develop a Relationship Over time, develop your relationship by participating in activities together, watching each other's movies, leaving supportive comments, and regularly communicating. As trust grows, consider adding one another as friends on social media platforms if appropriate. Guidelines: Always maintain a healthy balance between online interactions and real-life responsibilities. Additionally, remember to respect boundaries and never pressure anyone into advancing the relationship at a pace they're uncomfortable with. Step 8: Officially Become Boyfriends When both parties feel ready, officially become boyfriends within the game by stating your intentions clearly and openly. You may choose to celebrate this milestone by hosting a party, exchanging gifts, or simply enjoying quality time together in-game. Remember, every relationship is unique and should be built upon mutual respect, honesty, and clear communication. By following these steps and maintaining a positive attitude, you can successfully add a boyfriend on MovieStarPlanet and form lasting connections with others. Title: How to Convince Your Parents to Gift You Robux Introduction: Roblox is an online game platform that allows users to create their own games or play those created by others. The currency used within this virtual world is called Robux, which can be earned through various means such as selling items you've created, trading with other players, or purchasing them directly from Roblox using real money. If you want your parents to gift you some Robux but aren't sure how to approach the subject, follow these steps carefully. Remember, being respectful and considerate will increase your chances of success! Step 1: Understand Their Perspective Before asking your parents for anything, especially when it involves spending money, try to understand where they are coming from. They might have concerns about screen time, online safety, or financial responsibility. Acknowledging these worries shows maturity and understanding, making it more likely for them to listen to your request. Key Tip: Make a list of potential objections they may raise so you can prepare thoughtful responses ahead of time. Step 2: Research Responsibly Familiarize yourself with the basics of Robux - what it is, how it works, and why you need it. However, avoid focusing solely on the fun aspects; also highlight the educational benefits of Roblox, like problem-solving skills, creativity, and even coding opportunities if applicable. Guideline: Use reliable sources like official Roblox websites or articles written by reputable gaming blogs to gather information. Step 3: Present Your Case Choose the right moment to discuss your desire for Robux calmly and rationally. Explain clearly why you think getting Robux would benefit you (e.g., enhancing gameplay experience, encouraging creative expression). Be sincere and specific about what you hope to achieve in the game using the additional resources. Key Tip: Avoid using emotional manipulation tactics ("all my friends have loads of Robux...") as this could negatively impact their decision. Step 4: Show Financial Literacy Demonstrating knowledge about money management can reassure your parents that you take fiscal responsibilities seriously. Propose a budget plan detailing how much Robux you're asking for, what you intend to do with it, and how you plan to earn or save up for future purchases. Guideline: Suggest contributing part of your allowance or earnings from chores towards buying Robux to show initiative and commitment. Step 5: Address Safety Measures Reassure your parents about the safety features available on Roblox. Mention parental controls, reporting mechanisms, and privacy settings that ensure a secure gaming environment. Offer to explore these options together to strengthen their trust in the platform. Key Tip: Share any positive news stories or testimonials related to Roblox to further emphasize its safety. Step 6: Negotiate & Agree on Terms Listen carefully to their response, whether it's approval, disapproval, or conditions attached. If they deny your request, ask for reasons politely and accept their answer graciously. If there are conditions set, negotiate terms acceptable to both parties. Guideline: Always remember, it's okay to disagree sometimes. What matters most is maintaining open communication channels with your parents. Conclusion: Asking your parents for something you want takes patience, preparation, and understanding. By following these steps, you'll not only improve your chances of receiving Robux but also learn valuable lessons in communication, persuasion, and responsible gaming. Um I don't know I mean I don't think I've ever given her anything that looked like that. Lynx, a member of the cat family found in temperate and colder areas of the Northern Hemisphere. The lynx is valued for its warm, lightweight fur. Most lynxes are grayish-brown in color, often spotted or streaked with black. They have short bodies, stumpy tails, and tufted ears. There are four species of lynx: the Canada lynx, the Spanish lynx, the bobcat, and the caracal. The Canada lynx is found in forests throughout Canada and in the northern United States. It is 30 to 40 inches (75 to 100 cm) in length, including a 4-inch (10-cm) tail. It is 20 to 24 inches (50 to 60 cm) at the shoulder and weighs 25 to 40 pounds (11 to 18 kg). The Canada lynx is distinguished by its prominent ear tufts, black-tipped tail, and furred paws. It has keen eyesight. The Canada lynx is an agile climber and a good swimmer. It sleeps in rock crevices and caves during the day and hunts at night. Its main source of food is the snowshoe hare, but it also eats rodents and birds. One to four cubs are born about two months after mating. They nurse for about three months, and stay with the parents for up to nine months. The male helps feed the cubs. The Spanish lynx is yellowish-red with black spots. It was once found throughout Spain but, because of the clearing of land for farming, it is now restricted to a mountainous region in southwestern Spain. The Spanish lynx is an endangered species; fewer than 1,500 individuals are believed to exist in the wild. The Canada lynx is Felis lynx; The Spanish lynx. F. pardina. They belong to the cat family, Felidae. Title: How to Adopt a Working Dog Introduction: Working dogs are canines that have been trained to perform specific tasks, such as guiding the blind, search-and-rescue missions, or providing therapy services. These animals possess unique skills and temperaments compared to traditional pets. If you're considering adopting a working dog, this comprehensive guide will walk you through the process, offering valuable insights at every step. Step 1: Research Different Types of Working Dogs Before diving into adoption, familiarize yourself with various types of working dogs. This includes service dogs (guide, mobility assistance, hearing), police K9 units, search-and-rescue dogs, herding dogs, detection dogs (drugs, explosives, termites), therapy dogs, and sled dogs. Understanding their roles will help determine which breed is best suited for your lifestyle and needs. Key Tip: Each type of working dog requires different levels of training, exercise, and attention. Make sure you understand these requirements before proceeding. Step 2: Assess Your Capabilities Consider whether you can provide the necessary environment, time commitment, and financial support required by a working dog. Factors include living space (homes with yards preferred over apartments), physical ability to manage large breeds, availability for regular exercises, and budget for food, veterinary care, grooming, and equipment. Guideline: A typical working dog may live up to 10 years, so ensure your decision aligns with your long-term plans. Step 3: Locate Reputable Organizations Look for organizations specializing in placing retired or rescued working dogs. Examples include but aren't limited to Guide Dog Foundation, Paws With a Cause, and Canine Companions for Independence. Always verify their credentials and check reviews from previous adopters. Step 4: Apply for Adoption Fill out application forms provided by the chosen organization. Be honest about your expectations, capabilities, and limitations. Most applications involve background checks, references, home visits, and interviews. Remember, the goal is finding a perfect match for both parties. Step 5: Meet Potential Matches Once approved, schedule meetings with potential matches based on recommendations made by the organization. Treat this like a job interview - ask questions, observe behavior, assess compatibility. Allow the dog to interact with other family members too. Step 6: Prepare Your Home Prioritize safety measures, especially if children or existing pets are part of the household. Install secure fencing, remove toxic plants, store hazardous materials out of reach, etc. Also, gather essential supplies including leashes, collars, toys, beds, bowls, and appropriate food. Step 7: Post-Adoption Training & Socialization Plan for ongoing obedience training post-adoption. Enroll in group classes or hire a professional trainer who specializes in working dogs. Regular socialization opportunities are crucial; expose your new companion to diverse environments, sounds, smells, people, and animals gradually. Step 8: Maintain Records Keep records related to vaccinations, deworming treatments, health screenings, certifications, and training progress. Having all relevant documents organized makes future appointments easier and demonstrates responsibility during travel or emergencies. Conclusion: Bringing a working dog into your life brings joy, companionship, purpose, and satisfaction. However, remember that responsible ownership entails understanding, patience, dedication, and resources. By following these steps meticulously, you set the stage for a fulfilling relationship built on mutual respect and love. yeah, she got a pretty one with a dress on. Aboriginal people did not usually build houses, except huts of grass, leaves and bark. They did not usually build walls or fences, and there were no horses, cows or sheep in Australia that needed to be kept in pens. The only Aboriginal buildings that are known are fish-traps made from stones piled up in the river, and the remains of a few stone huts in Victoria and Tasmania. The Aboriginal people did not use metal or make pottery or use bows and arrows or weave cloth. In some parts of Australia the people used sharp flaked-stone spearheads, but most Aboriginal spears were made of sharply pointed wood. Australia has a lot of trees that have very hard wood that was good for spear making. The boomerang was used in some areas for sport and for hunting. huh, so what did the lady and the policeman do? Grade Levels: Pre-K - 2 - Children will practice their observation skills. - Children will learn about dental health. - 4 raw eggs - 4 clear cups - Other liquids, such as vinegar or milk - Break children into groups of four and distribute the eggs and the cups. - Ask children to place each egg in a different cup, and cover it with one of the above liquids. - At the beginning of the day, for four days in a row, ask children to observe the eggs and write down any changes they see in them. - After a few days, remove the eggs and encourage children to describe what happened to them and why. - Ask children: - How are teeth like eggs? - What will happen to your teeth if you drink cola, tea and coffee all the time? - Explain to children that the enamel on teeth is stained by certain liquids, but brushing them can remove stains and help prevent decay. - As extension activities, do the following: - Have children gently brush the egg with toothpaste and a toothbrush to see what happens. - Soak the eggs in other liquids and let children predict what will happen. - Invite a dentist to talk to the class about dental health and why it is important to brush frequently. - Make a list of foods that are not good for your teeth. - Ask the children to look in the mirror and count the number of teeth in their mouths. [to MOT about Fei wanting her own page to select] Chemistry Experiment: Mixing Liquid Solutions! Have you ever tried mixing different drinks together? Sometimes it creates a new flavor, but have you noticed that the resulting mixture has its own strength or concentration? That's exactly what we will learn today - how to calculate the concentration when we mix two liquid solutions! Let's imagine our liquids as two types of juice - let's call them Juice P and Juice Q. Each juice has a certain "pressure", which represents its concentration. Juice P has a pressure of 80 units (we'll call them "torr") while Juice Q has a pressure of 60 torr. Now, if we combine 3 bottles of Juice P with 2 bottles of Juice Q, it forms a single mixed solution. How do we determine the total "pressure" now? To figure this out, scientists use something called "mole fractions". Think of these like ratios of the number of bottles of each type of juice. In this case, since there were 3 bottles of Juice P and 2 bottles of Juice Q, our mole fractions are Xp = 3/5 (for Juice P), and Xq = 2/5 (for Juice Q). Now, here comes the fun part! To get the total "pressure" (or concentration) of our mixed solution, we multiply the mole fraction of each juice by their individual pressures (concentrations), then add those results together: Total Pressure = ((Mole Fraction of P) x (Pressure of P)) + ((Mole Fraction of Q) x (Pressure of Q)) So in our example: Total Pressure = (3/5 x 80) + (2/5 x 60) Total Pressure = (120 / 5) + (120 / 5) Total Pressure = 24 + 24 Total Pressure = 48 + 24 Total Pressure = 72 torr And voila, just like adding numbers, combining concentrations is easy once you know the trick! By understanding how to work with mole fractions and calculating combined concentrations, you begin your journey into more complex chemistry concepts. Keep learning, enjoy science experiments, and maybe even create some tasty mixed juice combinations of your own along the way! Wonder what happened to the frogs don't they? The structure of Earth changes from the inside to the outside. The center of earth (Earth's core) is mostly iron (88.8%), nickel (5.8%), sulfur (4.5%), and less than 1% other things. The Earth's crust is largely oxygen (47%). Oxygen is normally a gas but it can join with other chemicals to make compounds like water and rocks. 99.22% of rocks have oxygen in them. The most common oxygen-having rocks are silica (made with silicon), alumina (made with aluminium), rust (made with iron), lime (made with calcium), magnesia (made with magnesium), potash (made with potassium), and sodium oxide, and there are others as well. yeah, 'n' bugs 'n' little tiny bugs giraffe. Title: How to Delete a Draft in Gmail Deleting a draft in Gmail is a straightforward process that can be completed in just a few steps. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting a draft in Gmail, providing detailed explanations for each step and highlighting key tips and guidelines along the way. Step 1: Open your web browser and go to the Gmail website (<https://www.gmail.com>). Enter your email address and password to log in to your account. Step 2: Once you are logged in, locate the "Gear" icon in the upper right-hand corner of the screen. Click on this icon to open the Settings menu. Step 3: From the Settings menu, select "See all settings." This will take you to the main Settings page where you can customize various aspects of your Gmail account. Step 4: On the Settings page, click on the "Advanced" tab at the top of the screen. Scroll down to the "Drafts" section and make sure that the option labeled "Enable undo send" is turned on. This feature allows you to recall an email within a certain time frame after sending it, which can come in handy if you accidentally delete a draft before saving or sending it. Step 5: After ensuring that the "Undo Send" feature is enabled, return to the main Gmail interface by clicking on the "Inbox" link at the top left-hand side of the screen. Step 6: Locate the draft that you wish to delete in the list of emails displayed on the left-hand side of the screen. You may need to scroll down to find the draft if there are several other messages in your inbox. Step 7: Once you have found the draft, hover your mouse over it until a series of icons appear to the right of the message. One of these icons should resemble a pencil; this is the edit button. Click on this button to open the draft in a new window. Step 8: Review the contents of the draft one final time to ensure that you do indeed want to delete it. If you're confident that this is the correct action, look for the "Discard" button located near the bottom of the screen. It may also be represented as a trashcan icon. Step 9: Upon clicking the "Discard" button, a confirmation dialog box will appear asking if you are sure you want to permanently delete the draft. Select "OK" to confirm the deletion. Alternatively, if you change your mind, simply close the dialog box without taking any further action. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Always double-check the contents of a draft before discarding it, as deleted drafts cannot typically be recovered. * Enable the "Undo Send" feature to give yourself extra peace of mind when working with drafts. This feature provides a short grace period during which you can cancel sending or saving a draft, giving you more control over your outgoing communications. * Be aware that some third-party applications and extensions may interfere with Gmail's default functionality. If you experience issues while trying to delete a draft, consider disabling any such add-ons temporarily to see if they might be causing the problem. By following these simple steps and keeping the provided tips and guidelines in mind, you should now be able to successfully delete a draft in Gmail whenever necessary. Happy emailing! But soon, it began to rain! She quickly stepped back under a tall tree and waited for the rain to stop. When it finally did, the lady smiled and stepped off down the path. But she could not find a safe place to stay. Everywhere she stepped, the ground seemed to crumble under her feet. She tried to look for an old house or barn, but nothing was in sight. Finally, at the edge of the park, she spotted an old, crumbling stone wall. She stepped closer and, sure enough, there was an old, safe house hidden behind the wall. She quickly stepped through the door and settled in the snug and warm home. The old lady was safe from the rain and the inevitable thunder that would soon follow. She smiled and thanked the old, safe wall. [1w-7w mother screws the nipple back on the bottle] [mother and Nina are still looking at pictures in the abc book] [alternates moving each of the two puppets toward ROM] Title: How to Create Your Own Custom Nail Polish Introduction: Making your own nail polish can be a fun and creative way to express yourself. It allows you to control every aspect of the product, from color to consistency. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step so that you can create your perfect shade. Materials Needed: 1. Clear nail polish base or suspension base 2. Pigment powders (such as micas, oxides, ultramarines) 3. Mixing spoons or stir sticks 4. Small containers with lids (like empty pill bottles) 5. Glitter (optional) 6. Top coat sealant 7. Eye dropper (for transferring liquid) Step 1: Choose Your Base The first decision is what kind of clear nail polish base to use. There are two main types: a simple clear polish and a suspension base which keeps glitters suspended within the formula. If you plan on adding glitter to your custom color, choose the suspension base; otherwise, any regular clear polish will do. Key Tip: Always ensure that your chosen base has not expired, as this could lead to poor results or even health risks. Step 2: Select Your Pigments Pick out the pigment powders you want to mix together to form your unique color. These could include micas for shimmery finishes, oxides for deep tones, or ultramarines for bright blues and purples. Remember, a little goes a long way when dealing with pigment powders. Guideline: Start by adding small amounts of pigment, then gradually build up the intensity to avoid creating a color that's too dark or intense. Step 3: Combine Ingredients Pour some of your chosen base into one of your small containers. Add tiny increments of your selected pigments using a clean mixing spoon or stir stick until you reach your desired hue. Stir thoroughly after each addition to guarantee an even distribution of pigment throughout the mixture. Key Tip: Make sure there are no clumps of pigment left at the bottom of the container; if necessary, scrape down the sides and continue stirring. He's seen I think all of these before. Title: How to Have and Care for a Pet Rock Introduction: While having a pet rock may seem like an unconventional choice, it can be a fun and low-maintenance way to enjoy the companionship of a pet without the time commitment or financial burden associated with traditional pets. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about selecting, setting up, and caring for your new pet rock. Step 1: Choose Your Perfect Pet Rock Selecting the right rock is crucial as it sets the foundation for your relationship with your new pet. Here are some factors to consider when choosing your perfect pet rock: * Size: Decide where you'll display your pet rock - on a desk, bookshelf, or outdoor space? Pick a size that fits comfortably in its designated spot while still being large enough to appreciate. * Shape: Opt for a shape that appeals to you; rounded, angular, smooth, or textured rocks all make unique choices. * Color: From granite gray to sandy brown or even speckled green, choose a color scheme that complements your home decor. Key tip: Make sure to select a rock free from cracks or fragile areas to ensure longevity. Step 2: Prepare Its Habitat Creating an ideal environment for your pet rock involves finding a suitable container and adding necessary accessories: * Container: A glass aquarium, fishbowl, or clear plastic storage bin work well. The container should be deep enough so the rock doesn't roll around too much but shallow enough to allow easy viewing. * Bedding: Line the bottom of the container with natural materials such as pebbles, marbles, or colored river stones for added appeal. Avoid using materials like sand which could scratch or damage your rock over time. * Decorations (Optional): Add small plants, figurines, or other objects to create visual interest within the habitat. Be mindful not to clutter the space, keeping ample room for your pet rock to 'roam'. Step 3: Place Your Pet Rock Inside Carefully place your chosen rock into its newly prepared habitat. Arrange it atop the bedding, ensuring stability and balance. Allow your pet rock some time to adjust to its new surroundings before moving on to the next steps. Step 4: Clean Regularly Although less frequent than live animals, cleaning remains essential for maintaining your pet rock's habitat. Use warm water and mild soap to clean the container and replace old bedding periodically - every one to two months depending on usage. Guideline: Never submerge your pet rock in water during cleaning as this could lead to unwanted erosion or decay. Step 5: Display With Pride Find a prominent location where both you and others can admire your pet rock regularly. Consider placing it near a window ledge, office desk, or living area coffee table. Remember, your pet rock enjoys socializing just as much as any other pet! Step 6: Interact Daily Interaction plays a significant role in fostering bonding between owner and pet rock. Engage daily by gently rolling the rock back and forth, rearranging its position, or simply appreciating its presence. You might also name your pet rock if you haven't already done so. Conclusion: Having and caring for a pet rock requires minimal effort yet offers maximum satisfaction. By following these simple steps and incorporating regular interaction, you're bound to form a meaningful connection with your non-traditional companion. Enjoy the serenity and joy brought about by your newfound friendship! You know if you do it then you can go off the board anytime you want. But we height finish and do something else, honey. I, I, I have an Idea! Beginning to Read Literacy Mary Claire Sikes Rationale: This lesson teaches children about the long vowel correspondence i_ e = /I/. In order to be able to read, children must learn to recognize the spellings that map word pronunciations. In this lesson children will learn to recognize, spell, and read words containing the spelling i_ e = /I/. They will learn a meaningful representation (scientist saying "I, I, I have an idea") they will spell and read words containing this spelling in a letterbox lesson, and read a decodable book that focuses on the correspondence i_ e = /I/. Materials: Graphic image of scientist having an idea; cover up critter; whiteboard or smartboard Elkonin boxes for modeling and individual Elkonin boxes for each student; letter manipulatives for each child and magnetic or smart board letters for teacher: k, I, t, e, l, g, h, b, f, r, y, m, c; list of spelling words on poster or whiteboard to read: kite, light, bike, fire, fly, like, mice; decodable text: The Bike Ride, and assessment worksheet. 1. Say in order to become expert readers we need to learn the code that tells us how to pronounce words. We have already learned how to read short vowel words with i, like big, and today we are going to learn about long I and the silent e signal that is used to make I say its name, /I/. When I say /I/ I think of a scientist trying to say that he has an idea "I, I, I have an idea". When you're excited to tell someone something, do you repeat yourself? (show graphic image). Now let's look at the spelling of /I/ that we will learn today. One-way to spell /I/ is with the letter i and a signal e at the end of the word to tell me to say I's name. [Write i_e on the board.] This blank line here means there is a consonant after i, and at the end of the word there is a little silent e signal. The /I/ sound can also be spelled with the silent "gh" as in the word "sigh" or with a "y" and no "I" as in fly. 2. Say: Before we learn about the spelling of /I/, we need to listen for it in some words. When I listen for /I/ in words, I hear i say its name /I/ and my mouth opens and my jaw is open when I say the sound i. (Make vocal gestures for /I/.) I'll show you first: mine. I heard i say its name and my mouth opened with my jaw dropped to say i. There is a long I in mine. Now I'm going to see if it's in run. Hmmm, I didn't hear a say its name and my mouth didn't open with my jaw dropped. Now you try. If you hear /I/ say, I, I, I? If you don't hear /I/ say, "That's not it." Is it in snow, night, may, ride, cry, pain? (Have children drop your jaw with open mouths when they feel /I/ say its name.) 3. Now let's try spelling words using i_e= /I/. Break down the word kite. "I want to fly my kite in the park today." If we are going to spell kite in our letterboxes, we must first count the sounds we hear. Stretch it out with me: /k//I//t/. I need 3 letterboxes. I heard the /I/ after the /k/ so I will put I in the second letterbox. The word starts with /k/ so that goes in the first box and we already placed our I in the second box. What do we hear next? T. . .good. We need to put the T in the third box. Now let's sound out what we have: /k//i//t/ kit? What did we learn today to put at the end of a word to make a long I sound? Place that silent e outside the third box. . .right next to the T. Good. Let's look at our poster of words. Let's read the first one now that we know how to break down i_e= /I/. The first word is /b//I//k/. . .bike! "Let's go on a bike ride." 4. Say: Now I'm going to have you spell some words in letterboxes. You'll start out easy with two boxes for isle. An isle is a kind walkway or path, "To get to the front of the room we walked down the isle." What should go in the first box? [Respond to children's answers]. What goes in the second box? What about silent e, did you remember to put it outside the boxes? I'll check your spelling while I walk around the room. [Observe progress.] You'll need three letterboxes for the next word. Listen for the beginning sound to spell in the first box. Then listen for /I/ and don't forget to put the signal silent e at the end, outside the boxes. Here's the word: like, "I like to help my mom out with the dishes"; like. [Allow children to spell remaining words: light, fire, fly, mice] 5. Say: Now I am going to let you read the words you've spelled. [Have children read words in unison. Afterwards, call on individuals to read one word on the list until everyone has had a turn.] 6. Say: You've done a great job reading words with our new spelling for /I/: i_e. Now we are going to read a book called The Bike Ride. This is a story of two boys who are playing together but Tim wants to go on a hike but Nate does not. How can Tim encourage Nate to get outside and play with him? Let's pair up and take turns reading The Bike Ride to find out if Nate goes outside with Tim. [Children pair up and take turns reading alternate pages while teacher walks around the room monitoring progress. After individual paired reading, the class rereads The Bike Ride aloud together, and stops between pages to discuss the plot.] 7. Say: Before we finish up with our lesson about one way to spell /I/ = i_e, I want to see how you can solve a reading problem. On this worksheet we need to separate Mike's pictures from Mick's pictures. First say each picture out loud. Mike drew pictures with long I like in his name: Mike. Color these pictures. Mick drew pictures with short i like in his name: Mick. Circle these pictures. [evaluate students' progress by worksheets] Reference: Caroline Conner, http://www.auburn.edu/academic/education/reading_genie/doorways/connercbr.htm Constance Wood, http://www.auburn.edu/academic/education/reading_genie/doorways/woodcbr.htm Assessment worksheet: http://free-phonics-worksheets.com/html/phonics_worksheet_v1-38.html Return to Awakenings Index King Harold's army took up a position on an east-west ridge north of Hastings. Title: How to Guide a Teenager Introduction: Adolescence is a critical period of growth and development, both physically and emotionally. As a parent, teacher, or mentor, guiding a teenager through these years can be challenging but also incredibly rewarding. This guide will provide you with clear steps and useful tips to help you effectively support and direct a teenager during this important stage of their life. Step 1: Establish Trust and Open Communication Explanation: Building trust and fostering open communication are essential foundations for any successful guidance process. By creating an environment where the teenager feels comfortable sharing their thoughts, feelings, and experiences, you'll be better equipped to understand their perspectives and offer appropriate advice. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Listen actively when they speak, showing genuine interest and empathy. * Avoid being judgmental or dismissive of their concerns. * Encourage them to express themselves honestly by assuring confidentiality (unless safety is at risk). * Be approachable and available, making time for regular conversations. How It Helps Achieve Desired Outcome: When teenagers feel heard and understood, they're more likely to confide in you, seek your advice, and consider your input as they make decisions. Step 2: Set Clear Expectations and Boundaries Explanation: Clearly defined expectations and boundaries provide structure and promote responsible behavior. They help teenagers understand what is acceptable and unacceptable while offering guidance on navigating various aspects of their lives. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Discuss rules and consequences together, ensuring mutual understanding. * Tailor expectations according to the teenager's age, maturity level, and individual needs. * Regularly review and adjust expectations as necessary. How It Helps Achieve Desired Outcome: Clear expectations and boundaries create consistency, foster self-discipline, and contribute to building healthy relationships between adults and teenagers. Title: How to Fly Out of Boston Logan International Airport Boston Logan International Airport (BOS) is one of the busiest airports in the United States, serving millions of passengers every year. To ensure a smooth travel experience, here's an extensive guide on flying out of BOS with detailed steps, helpful tips, and crucial information. **Step 1: Prepare your documents and luggage** Before you even set foot in the airport, make sure you have all necessary documentation ready. This includes: * A valid government-issued photo ID, such as a driver's license or passport. For international flights, always carry a passport. Double-check its expiration date; some countries require at least six months of remaining validity. * Printed copies of your flight reservation(s), hotel booking confirmation(s), rental car voucher(s), and any other relevant travel documents. Although digital versions are usually accepted, having physical backups can be handy if there are connectivity issues. * Make a list of items prohibited from both carry-on and checked baggage according to TSA regulations (<https://www.tsa.gov/travel/security-screening/whatcanibring/all>). Be aware that individual airlines may also impose additional restrictions. Key tip: Pack essentials like medication, valuables, electronics, important papers, snacks, and reading materials in your carry-on luggage so they stay accessible during the journey. **Step 2: Check-in online** Most major airlines allow online check-ins starting 24 hours before departure. By doing this early, you save time at the airport and secure your preferred seat selection. Keep these points in mind: 1. Confirm your airline's specific check-in window. 2. Enter required details (e.g., name, confirmation number) to access your reservation. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in the rain and jump in puddles. Macbriar had scarce understood the purport of the words as first pronounced by the Lord President of the Council; but he was sufficiently recovered to listen and to reply to the sentence when uttered by the harsh and odious voice of the ruffian who was to execute it, and at the last awful words, "And this I pronounce for doom," he answered boldly-- "My Lords, I thank you for the only favour I looked for, or would accept at your hands, namely, that you have sent the crushed and maimed carcass, which has this day sustained your cruelty, to this hasty end. It were indeed little to me whether I perish on the gallows or in the prison-house; but if death, following close on what I have this day suffered, had found me in my cell of darkness and bondage, many might have lost the sight how a Christian man can suffer in the good cause. For the rest, I forgive you, my Lords, for what you have appointed and I have sustained--And why should I not?--Ye send me to a happy exchange--to the company of angels and the spirits of the just, for that of frail dust and ashes--Ye send me from darkness into day--from mortality to immortality--and, in a word, from earth to heaven!--If the thanks, therefore, and pardon of a dying man can do you good, take them at my hand, and may your last moments be as happy as mine!" The antidote of a poison will slow or reverse its effects. The antidote may itself be a poison. As an example, atropine can be used as an antidote against certain nerve gases, like tabun or sarin, or against certain insecticides. It is also used as a medication. In high doses, atropine is a poison. Yet atropine is a "core" medicine in the World Health Organization's "essential drugs list". Run your tongue over your teeth. If it's been a while since you last brushed, you may feel a filmy or fuzzy coating on your teeth. What's there is similar to the slimy coating you might feel if you stuck your finger down into a sink drain or that you might see coating the sides and bottom of a swimming pool that hasn't been kept clean. These are all examples of biofilms. Biofilms are communities of microbes. They form when bacterial cells able to make large amounts of sticky, slimy substances called polysaccharides (polly-sack-uh-rides) attach themselves to a surface. The slimy coating they make holds the cells to the surface they've settled on and captures other bacteria who live and grow off the waste products produced by the first bacteria. Layers keep being added, creating a complex community. In this activity, you'll explore whether biofilms can form on all possible surfaces and which they grow best on. You'll also learn something about how we can prevent biofilm formation. Print out these pages and follow the directions to do this activity at home. When you're done, come back to this page to test your newfound knowledge by answering the questions below. (No fair peeking at the answers before you do the activity!) Note: This is a long-term activity that will stretch over a several days. Note: Be careful handling sharp scissors. 1. Create an artificial pond: Fill your large bucket with tap water, place it on newspaper and let it sit for 24 hours. Pour 2 cups of dirt or mud into the bucket and stir in. Make sure the dirt is as free of chemicals as possible (e.g. don't use dirt from a garden treated with weed or insect killers). Or if you have a stream or pond nearby, you could just fill a bucket with water and dirt from there. Be very careful around bodies of water that are or may be deep. Fill your bucket near the edge and don't go into the water. Wash your hands when done. 2. Using the scissors, cut the top and bottom off your soda bottle about 5 cm from each end. Throw away the top and bottom. The remaining part of the bottle looks like a cylinder. Cut through it from top to bottom to create a rectangular piece. Flatten the piece and then cut into 5 equal strips. Punch a hole into each end of each strip. 3. Set one strip aside. Spread newspaper or paper towels on a table. Working over the newspaper, coat each of the other 4 strips, front and back, with a different coating. Use only one coating substance for each strip. Set them on the newspaper and let them dry (drying time will vary for each coating substance used). 4. Arrange your 5 strips in a row so that they are parallel. Thread a piece of string through the hole in one end of one strip and pull it through, leaving about about 7 cm hanging. Tie the strip to the string. Thread the long end of the string through one hole of the next strip and tie it off about 3 cm from the first strip. Continue to thread the strips onto the string, keeping them 3 cm apart. Using your second string, repeat this process on the other side of the the 5 strips. Tie whatever you're using as weights (washers, fishing weights, stones) to the 7 cm ends of the strings. Tie the other ends of your strings together. Your setup should look like the picture here. 5. Hang your setup from the stick or dowel and lay the stick across the top of your artificial pond so that the setup hangs down into the water. All strips should be submerged, but they shouldn't be resting on the bottom. Wash your hands. Leave the bucket and setup undisturbed for two weeks (check occasionally to make sure your top strips are still covered by water). 6. Working carefully around your setup so you don't jostle it badly, remove a few cups of water from your artificial pond and pour into your shallow pan or small bucket placed on newspaper. Carefully move your setup to this pan or bucket so that it doesn't dry out while you're working with it. You'll likely see green, brown, pink or reddish spots. Rate the amount of growth on each strip on a scale of 1 to 5 (1 being the least growth). Give the uncoated strip a 3 rating and rate the other strips compared to it. Create a bar graph with the different coatings labeled on the horizontal axis and the growth ratings on the vertical axis. Wash your hands after touching strips. If you want more details about biofilms and to see an animation showing how biofilms form, visit this Web site. Let s Play a Game |Grades||3 - 5| |Activity Time||One hour to create the game and then time to play| - Blank file folders, posterboard, or other sturdy paper - Colored paper, markers, crayons, glue, scissors - Note cards - Envelopes or zipper-seal bags - Paper clips - Items that can be used as tiles, such as small cardboard or posterboard cutouts - Sample Circular Board Design - Sample Tile Design - Sample Triangle Board Design - Working together, decide what kind of game will be created. Think about and share what kind of games you like to play and how you can make your own. You can also look at a list of games and their rules to get some ideas. - Most games have a purpose-what will be the purpose of your game? Will the winner capture the most pieces? Does the hero need to get to the heroine? Do you want the highest score or the lowest? - Collect all of the needed supplies. - If making a board game: - Open the folder and draw the game board. You can also glue one of the sample board designs to the inside of the folder or posterboard. The layout should cover as much of the inside of the sturdy paper as it can with the game board. - Write the name of the game on the folder tab. - If creating a card game or dice game, plan what will be on the cards and/or the recording sheets. - If making a game with tiles, write letters, numbers or words on them to fit into the game. - Once the board or other materials have been created, write the game's instructions. Include a title, materials needed, game play steps, and any diagrams that will help people play your game. - Create additional game pieces and question cards if needed. Paper clips with colored paper attached to them make great player markers. - Decorate the front cover of the folder with the game title and other pictures and images. - Place all game pieces and question cards in envelopes. Zipper-seal bags attached to the game board with tape also make great pockets to hold question cards and game pieces. - Once the game has been designed and created, play it to see if everything makes sense. If not, make adjustments until the game can be played. - Play and have fun! - Boxes or circles could be arranged to form pathways. - One large circle could be formed for players to work their way toward the middle. - Games can be based on children's favorite games: Chutes and Ladders, Monopoly, Yahtzee, or Scrabble. - The game can be based on a favorite book, TV show, or movie. [points to all the wooden people whom she has put to sleep] Title: How to Get Scene Hair if You Have Naturally Curly and Frizzy Hair Scene hair is characterized by its straight, sleek appearance with sharp angles, choppy layers, and bold colors. If you have naturally curly or frizz-prone hair, achieving this look can be challenging. However, with patience, practice, and the right techniques, you too can rock scene hairstyles. This guide will walk you through ten steps to help you transform your unruly locks into smooth, edgy tresses. ### Step 1: Shampoo and Condition Properly Start by washing your hair with a smoothing shampoo and conditioner that are designed to combat frizz. Look for products containing ingredients like argan oil, keratin, or silicones to add moisture and protect your hair from humidity. Avoid using hot water as it opens up the cuticle, leading to more frizz; opt for lukewarm instead. Gently squeeze out excess water after rinsing but do not rub your hair with a towel, which causes friction and increases frizziness. Instead, blot your hair gently with a microfiber towel or an old cotton T-shirt. Key tip: Don't wash your hair every day, as over-shampooing strips natural oils from your scalp, causing dryness and making frizz worse. Aim for every other day or even three times per week. ### Step 2: Apply Heat Protectant Before blow drying or heat styling your hair, always apply a quality heat protectant product. These sprays create a barrier between your strands and the heat source, preventing damage and reducing frizz. Spray liberally onto damp hair, focusing especially on the ends where breakage tends to occur most often. ### Step 3: Detangle Carefully Curly and frizzy hair is prone to tangles, so carefully detangling before proceeding any further is crucial. Use a wide-tooth comb or a detangling brush made specifically for wet hair. Start at the bottom and work your way up slowly, being gentle to avoid pulling out any knots or breaking delicate strands. Key guideline: Never use regular brushes on wet hair, as they cause unnecessary tugging and lead to breakage and split ends. ### Step 4: Section Your Hair Can you get the rest for mamma? Title: How to Change the Twitter Alert Sound on an iPhone Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing the alert sound for your Twitter app notifications on your iPhone. By default, Twitter uses the same tone as other system alerts, but with this simple process, you can customize it according to your preference. This change allows you to differentiate between various apps' notifications at first glance or even while your phone is in silent mode, improving functionality and personalization. Step 1: Open Settings App Locate and tap on the grey gear icon labeled 'Settings', typically found on your home screen or within a folder. The settings app controls all aspects related to device configuration, including sounds and notification preferences. Step 2: Scroll Down & Tap 'Sounds & Haptics' Once inside the Settings app, scroll down until you see 'Sounds & Haptics'. It is usually located towards the bottom half of the page among other general settings categories. Tapping here opens the audio control center where users manage volume levels, ringtones, vibration patterns, etc. Step 3: Understand Sounds & Haptics Interface The 'Sounds & Haptics' interface consists of several sections like 'Sound Effects', 'Ringtones', 'Text Tone', 'Vibration', and more. Familiarizing yourself with these options provides insight into overall iOS sound management, enabling better decision-making when setting up unique alert tones. Step 4: Locate & Tap 'Twitter' under 'Sound Effects' Section After familiarizing yourself with the layout, look for 'Twitter' under the 'SOUND EFFECTS' section. Each application that has specific assigned sounds (including mail, messages, calendar reminders) appears listed here. If no custom sound has been set previously, the current selection would be 'Default'. Step 5: Choose New Alert Sound Upon selecting 'Twitter', you are directed to a list titled 'Select a New Sound for Notifications'. Here, browse through diverse built-in tunes by scrolling vertically. Listen to any potential choice before deciding by pressing play next to its name; a blue circle with a white arrow indicates playback availability. Key Tip: Select a distinctive sound from others so you won't confuse them later. Step 6: Confirm Selection & Test After choosing a preferred sound, ensure it's highlighted then hit 'Back' located at the top left corner of the display. You'll return to the previous menu confirming your chosen sound is now displayed beside 'Twitter'. To verify if everything works correctly, send a test tweet or ask someone else to mention you on Twitter. Your device should emit the newly configured sound upon receiving the new activity notice. Conclusion: Congratulations! With these steps, you have successfully changed your Twitter alert sound on your iPhone. Customizing such details enhances user experience making interaction enjoyable yet productive. Remember, clear distinctions between app notifications reduce response time leading to improved communication efficiency. [Nina goes over to her shelf to look for other farm animals]. Phoenicopteriformes are also known as flamingos. Flamingos have long legs so that means that they can go into deeper water level. They are found at the Western and Eastern Hemisphere. Flamingos do not sleep like other birds. They sleep sitting down with their feet tucked in, or standing on one leg. While they sleep, flamingos like to face the wind. In fact, when you see them sleeping, they tend to move back and forth because of the wind. Flamingos always stand on one leg and curl the leg under the body to keep their foot warm and to conserve their body heat. Whether the weather is cool or warm, the flamingos still curl their legs. Before a flamingo takes off, they first run several steps, begin to flap their wings, and they lift up into the air. Landing is reversed. The bird touches down and runs several paces. When a flamingo is in flight, their head and neck is stretched out in front and its legs right behind it. The flight speed of a flock of flamingos can reach 50 to 60 kph (31-37mph) they are known to fly 500 to 600 km (311-373mi.) Blue-green and red algae, diatoms, larval and adult forms of small insects, crustaceans, mollusks, and small fishes are the main diet for flamingos. The shape of a flamingo's bill determines its diet. It will either have a shallow or a deep-keeled-bill. They fight over the food they eat because of feeding grounds. When flamingos are born, they are grayish white color. The chicks have straight beaks which will become curved as they mature. The adults feed them food from glands located near the digestive tracks. It takes three years for the chicks to get full coloration. Flamingos will not display or breed until they get their full pink color. Some flamingos will abandon their nests because of a low flying aircraft. They are also noisy and social birds. They take flight as they sense a sudden movement or noise. Flamingos can live up to fifty years. What should we play today asked the camel with the wrinkled knees? Do you want to come up and have cookies with me? After school, Lily went back to the ranch and told her mommy what happened. Her mommy gave her a big hug and said, "Don't worry, Lily. It's okay to make mistakes. We all have accidents sometimes." Lily felt better and went back to playing in the fields, happy to be home where she felt safe and loved. Med would you like to see something else? I'm going to cut the back oh Mommy. An important concept that comes from sequences is that of series and summation. Series and summation describes the addition of terms of a sequence. There are different types of series, including arithmetic and geometric series. Series and summation follows its own set of notation that is important to memorize in order to understand homework problems. So a series is just the summation of a sequence. So a sequence is just a bunch of numbers in a row, a series is what happens when we add up all those numbers together. Okay? So before me I have a general term for a sequence. a sub n is equal to n squared minus 1. And first we're asked to find the first four terms. Okay? So in order to find the first term, we would find a sub 1 which happens when we plug in 1. 1 squared minus 1 that's just 0. So our first term is going to be 0. To find the second term we plug in 2. a sub 2 is equal to 2 squared. 4-1 which is going to give us 3. Third term [IB] and repeat a sub 3 is 3 squared, 9-1 is 8. And the fourth term a sub 4, plug in 4. 4 squared, 16-1 is 15. So this right here is a sequence. It's 4 numbers written in order with commons in between. It's just a collection of numbers. Find the sum of those first 4 terms. So basically we already found the 4 terms, all we have to do is add them together. 0+3 is 3 plus 8 is 11 plus 15 is 26. So 26 is then the series, okay? Series is the way I remember it is, series is a shorter word therefore your answer should be shorter, one number. A sequence is a longer word, it's going to be a collection of data, a collection of numbers, okay? So basically all the series is is a summation of the sequence. Between October 2005 and 28 February 2006, the BBC offered a service called the BBC iPlayer on their website - bbc.co.uk. It allowed people to catch up on the last seven days of TV and radio on the BBC. Users could either watch (stream) it or download the content on their computers. The downloading options are for Microsoft Windows computers and Apple devices. The iPlayer service was released to the public on 25 December 2007. Students are learning more about word meaning, and how the meaning of a word can change when a consonant suffix (-s) is added or vowel consonant suffix is added (-es). Students are also learning the rules of spelling words with suffix, and when to use -s or -es. The suffix rule is: use -es at the end of a word when the word ends in ch, sh, x, s, or z. For example: watch - watches, dish - dishes, mix - mixes. We played a fun guessing game today where children needed to choose which suffix to add at the end of a word... we used reporter's notebooks to write our guesses, which was especially important since what the word looks like helps students remember the suffix rule! Termites, though sometimes called "white ants", are not ants and belong to the order Isoptera. Termites are actually more closely related to cockroaches and mantids. Termites are eusocial but differ greatly in the genetics of reproduction. The similar social structure is attributed to convergent evolution. Title: How to French Braid Your Own Hair Difficulty: Moderate Time Required: 10-15 minutes ### Things Needed * A brush or comb * Hair tie or elastic band * Mirror (optional but recommended) ### Before You Start French braiding your own hair can be quite challenging at first, especially if you are new to braiding. It may take some practice before you get the hang of it. Be patient with yourself and remember that it's okay to make mistakes - they're part of the learning process! Here are some helpful tips to keep in mind as you begin: 1. Practice makes perfect: Don't worry if your first few attempts don't turn out exactly as you want them to. Keep trying and soon you'll find that creating a beautiful French braid becomes second nature. 2. Use a mirror: While it is possible to french braid without one, using a mirror will greatly improve your ability to see what you're doing and ensure a neater result. 3. Brush your hair thoroughly: Remove any tangles or knots from your hair before starting the braid to make the process smoother and more comfortable. 4. Divide sections evenly: Make sure each section of hair being used in the braid is roughly equal in size; this will help maintain an attractive appearance throughout the length of the braid.
