Thus being undiscovered by any, they departed thence, and arrived at home in convenient time, where being alone by themselves in the Chamber: she washed the head over and over with her teares, and bestowed infinite kisses thereon. Not long after, the Nurse having brought her a large earthen potte, such as wee use to set Basile, Marjerom, Flowers, or other sweet hearbes in, and shrouding the head in a silken Scarfe, put it into the pot, covering it with earth, and planting divers rootes of excellent Basile therein, which she never watered, but either with her teares, Rose water, or water distilled from the Flowers of Oranges. This pot she used continually to sitte by, either in her chamber, or any where elsee: for she caried it alwaies with her, sighing and breathing foorth sad complaints thereto, even as if they had beene uttered to her _Lorenzo_, and day by day this was her continuall exercise, to the no meane admiration of her bretheren, and many other friends that beheld her. So long she held on in this mourning manner, that, what by the continuall watering of the Basile, and putrifaction of the head, so buried in the pot of earth; it grew very flourishing, and most odorifferous to such as scented it, so that as no other Basile could possibly yeeld so sweet a savour. They can cause mild irritation of the liver. They can rarely cause very bad liver damage. Because of this, when someone starts taking a statin, doctors check liver blood tests after six weeks. Martin Gardner gives several examples of this, including a reading Cayce did for his own wife, who had tuberculosis: Title: How to Perform the "Royal Vacation" Card Trick The Royal Vacation card trick is an impressive feat that involves moving four kings to the top of the deck while maintaining the appearance of shuffling the cards. This seemingly simple yet baffling magic trick requires some practice but can be mastered with patience and persistence. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to perform this captivating illusion. **Step 1: Preparing the Deck** Before starting the trick, secretly arrange the four kings at the bottom of the deck. Make sure they are perfectly aligned so you can quickly locate them during the performance. This prearrangement sets up the foundation for executing the main move successfully. It might take several attempts to get used to setting up the deck discreetly without attracting attention. *Key Tip:* Practice arranging the kings until you feel comfortable doing it swiftly and unobtrusively. **Step 2: The Cover Story** Engage your audience by telling a story about taking a vacation with royalty (the kings). Explain that these royals love traveling incognito among commoners like us, hence their position at the bottom of the pack. Asking someone from the crowd to cut the deck after hearing the tale adds authenticity and mystery to the act. *Guideline:* Be confident and engaging when narrating the cover story; it creates suspense and makes the trick more enjoyable. **Step 3: False Shuffle** Perform a false shuffle known as a Hindu Shuffle or Faro Shuffle. While making it look as if you're thoroughly mixing the cards, ensure the four kings remain together at the bottom of the deck. There are numerous online resources available to learn this technique accurately. Mastering the false shuffle is crucial because it maintains the integrity of the trick while giving the impression that everything is fair and square. *Key Tips:* Keep practicing the false shuffle until you can do it smoothly without exposing the kings. Also, maintain eye contact with your audience throughout this process to distract them from focusing on the critical movement. **Step 4: Table Cut** Ask a spectator to place the halves back together, creating what appears to be a randomly reshuffled deck. However, due to your careful manipulation, the kings stay intact near the bottom. *Guideline:* Encourage the participant to mix the two parts evenly, which will help maintain the illusion. **Step 5: Second False Shuffle** Carry out another round of the Hindu Shuffle or Faro Shuffle, again preserving the sequence of the kings. Remember, deception lies at the heart of this trick, so make sure your actions appear genuine and spontaneous. *Key Point:* Maintain focus and control over the cards during the second shuffle since any mistakes could expose the setup. **Step 6: Deal Four Cards** Announce that you want to check whether the royals made it through safely. Proceed to deal four face-down cards onto the table, revealing the anticipated quartet of kings. Your audience should react with surprise and admiration! *Guidelines:* Slow down the pace slightly as you reveal the kings, emphasizing the climax of the trick. Smile and enjoy the moment, sharing in the delight of having pulled off something magical! In conclusion, performing the Royal Vacation card trick demands skillful sleight of hand, effective storytelling, and convincing misdirection. With dedication and consistent practice, you too can become proficient at astonishing others with this memorable illusion. Happy tricking! That blanket on so he could so you could so they wear... Minor chords (like Ami and Dmi), which sound sad. Dominant seventh chords (like G7 or D7), which sound slightly jazzy and seem to want to lead us to resolve to a major chord. Okay, now look at the E7 chord, illustrated in FIGURE 7-20. This chord is a part of the key (family) of A, which includes A and D. FIGURES 7-21 and 7-22 shows a couple of songs that use the E7 chord, which you can listen to on Tracks 36 and 37 of this book's CD. In FIGURES 7-23 are all the open-string chords you should learn. One of the things that makes learning chords easier is that families (keys) share the same chords, or variations on them. So once you know how to play a C chord, or an E7 chord, it will be the same regardless of the sequence you find it in. Buy some songbooks, or look on the Web for some song sheets you can download. Make sure they have the chords to the tunes printed on them so you can practice the songs. Spend some time working on changing smoothly from one chord to another as you strum. Some of these chords involve using your first finger to stop more than one string. To do this, use the fatty part of your finger. Initially, you may want to lay your second finger on top of your first finger to help you make just enough contact with the strings on the fret-board so the strings don't buzz. If you pay attention, you'll notice some chords in the same key are missing. That's because they can't be played with open strings. You will soon learn how to play these chords. Title: How to Hold Your Boyfriend's Hand Introduction: Holding hands is a simple yet intimate act that can strengthen your bond with your boyfriend. It shows affection, trust, and companionship. This tutorial will guide you through the process of holding your boyfriend's hand, providing detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Choose the Right Moment Timing is crucial when initiating hand-holding. Look for opportunities where both of you are relaxed and focused on each other, such as during a walk, movie, or quiet conversation. Avoid high-stress situations like driving, sports events, or crowded places since these might distract him from noticing or appreciating the gesture. Key Tip: Make sure your hands are clean and well-groomed before attempting to hold his hand. Good hygiene demonstrates respect and care for yourself and others. Step 2: Gauge His Comfort Level Before making any physical contact, pay attention to his body language and overall demeanor. If he seems tense, distant, or uninterested, it may be best to wait for another time. However, if he appears engaged and open, proceed to the next step. Guideline: Always obtain consent before engaging in physical touch. Respect his personal space and feelings, and never force him into anything he isn't comfortable with. Step 3: Extend Your Hand Once you have determined that the moment is right, extend your arm towards him with your palm facing upwards. This position signifies vulnerability and invites connection. Keep your movements slow and deliberate to avoid startling him. Key Tip: Maintain eye contact while extending your hand. This nonverbal cue conveys confidence and sincerity, increasing the likelihood that he will reciprocate the gesture. Step 4: Wait for His Response After offering your hand, patiently await his reaction. He may immediately take your hand, hesitate briefly, or look confused. Regardless of his response, remain calm and understanding. Give him time to process the situation and make a decision. Guideline: Never grab or pull his hand without permission. Forcing the issue could lead to discomfort or mistrust, damaging your relationship. Step 5: Interlock Fingers (Optional) If he accepts your offer by taking your hand, you can choose to interlock fingers as an additional show of intimacy. To do this, gently slide your fingers between his, ensuring they fit comfortably together. Be mindful of ring sizes and finger lengths to prevent causing pain or awkwardness. Key Tip: Pay attention to any signs of discomfort or unease, such as wincing, fidgeting, or pulling away. If noticed, release his hand and discuss any concerns openly. Step 6: Enjoy the Connection With your hands now joined, relax and enjoy the shared experience. Engage in meaningful conversation, appreciate nature during a walk, or simply bask in the warmth of his touch. Let this small but significant action reinforce your bond and deepen your connection. Guideline: Remember to maintain a firm yet gentle grip throughout the interaction. Overly tight or loose holds can cause discomfort or slippage, disrupting the moment. Conclusion: By following these steps and maintaining open communication, you can successfully hold your boyfriend's hand and create a deeper emotional connection. Practice patience, attentiveness, and respect to ensure a positive experience for both parties involved. This day is the first day of the last third of the year as well as the two thirds point of the common year because there are 243 days before and 121 days after it in common years. There are 244 days before and 121 after it in leap years. The exact time the second third of the year starts is 8:00 a.m. In countries that use summer time the actual exact time of the midpoint in a common year is at 9:00 a.m. for locations in the northern hemisphere, or 7:00 a.m. for locations in the southern hemisphere; this is when 243 days and 8 hours have elapsed and there are 121 days and 16 hours remaining. In a leap year in those countries, the last third of the year starts at midnight. In countries that use summer time, the second third starts at 1:00 a.m. on September, or 11:00 p.m. on August 31 in the southern hemisphere. This is due to summer time having advanced the time by one hour. But don't blow on it too hard you'll blow it right off the spoon like this [blowing]. I bet Gary will come with his great big van. The average temperature on Neptune is about -201 C (-331 F; 72 K). Therefore, Neptune is the coldest planet in the Solar System. But in the depths of planet the temperature rises slowly. The source of this heating is unknown. Neptune is the farthest planet from the Sun, yet its internal energy is strong enough to create the fastest winds seen in the Solar System. Several possible explanations have been suggested. Firstly, radiogenic heating from the planet's core. Among the explanations is the continued radiation into space of leftover heat made by infalling matter during the planet's birth. The last explanation is gravity waves breaking above the tropopause. Getting tabs on Internet Explorer can greatly enhance your browsing experience by allowing you to open multiple websites within a single window, making multitasking easier and more efficient. This tutorial will guide you through the process of enabling and using tabs in Internet Explorer with clear and detailed steps. **Part 1: Enabling Tabs in Internet Explorer** Step 1: **Open Internet Explorer** Start by launching Internet Explorer from your desktop or start menu. If you don't have Internet Explorer installed, download and install it first before proceeding further. Step 2: **Access Internet Options** Click on the gear icon located at the top-right corner of the browser window. A dropdown list will appear; select 'Internet options.' Key tip: *If you are using an older version of Internet Explorer (e.g., IE8), look for Tools instead of the Gear icon.* Step 3: **Go to the General Tab** Inside the new dialog box that appears, click on the 'General' tab if it is not already selected. It contains settings related to your homepage and browsing history. Step 4: **Check Enable Tabbed Browsing** Under the 'Tabs Settings' section, check the box next to 'Enable tabbed browsing.' Then hit Apply followed by OK. By doing this, you enable the feature that allows you to open and manage web pages as separate tabs inside one Internet Explorer window. Guideline: *Restart Internet Explorer after changing these settings so they take effect.* **Part 2: Using Tabs in Internet Explorer** Now let us explore some basic functions of managing tabs in Internet Explorer. Step 5: **Open New Tab** To create a new tab, either press Ctrl+T simultaneously on your keyboard or click on the "+". Icon located near the last opened tab. You can also right-click anywhere on the tab bar and choose 'New Tab'. Step 6: **Switch Between Tabs** You can quickly move between tabs without clicking them directly. Press Ctrl+Tab to cycle forward among all tabs or Ctrl+Shift+Tab to go backward. Alternatively, click the leftmost or rightmost arrows beside the "+" button to navigate sequentially. Step 7: **Close Current Tab** When you want to close the current tab, simply click on the 'X' mark present on every active tab or press Ctrl+W shortcut keys. Remember that closing the last tab will shut down the entire application unless there are other background windows running. Step 8: **Reopen Closed Tab** Accidentally closed a vital tab? No worries! Just right-click on any tab and then select 'Reopen closed tab', or tap Ctrl+Shift+T. But note that only the most recently closed tab can be recovered. Step 9: **Pin Tabs** For frequently visited sites, pinning their respective tabs might come handy. Right-click on a tab and choose 'Pin tab'. The favicon of the site will replace the text label and stick itself to the far left side of the tab row even when switching among different tabs. To unpin, repeat the same action but now opt for 'Unpin tab'. Remembering these simple yet powerful shortcuts and features makes navigating with tabs much faster and convenient. Enjoy seamless multi-tasking while browsing with Internet Explorer! Where'd daddy say he was going to take you? An' there's the napkin I'll put over by the tape recorder? [looks in the pocket for more clothes] [handing him his own pencil which is beginning of game of swapping pencils] Title: How to Properly Enjoy Chocolate Introduction: Chocolate is one of the most popular treats worldwide, enjoyed by people of all ages. However, many people don't take the time to truly savor and enjoy this delectable delight. This tutorial will guide you through the proper way to enjoy chocolate, ensuring that every bite is a sensory experience. By following these steps, you can appreciate the complex flavors and textures that different types of chocolate have to offer. Step 1: Choose Your Chocolate The first step to enjoying chocolate is selecting the right type. There are various kinds of chocolate available, such as dark, milk, and white. Each has its unique flavor profile and texture. Dark chocolate contains higher cocoa solids content (usually above 70%), making it more bitter but also richer in antioxidants. Milk chocolate includes dairy products like milk powder or condensed milk, giving it a creamier taste and smoother texture. White chocolate doesn't contain any cocoa solids; instead, it consists mainly of cocoa butter, sugar, and milk solids. Key Tip: Opt for high-quality chocolates with minimal ingredients for maximum flavor. Step 2: Store Your Chocolate Correctly Proper storage ensures your chocolate retains its optimal flavor and texture. Keep it away from direct sunlight, heat sources, and strong odors. Ideally, store chocolate between 60 F - 68 F (15 C - 20 C) with humidity below 60%. If stored correctly, solid chocolate can last up to two years without losing much quality. Guideline: Avoid refrigerating chocolate unless necessary, as temperature fluctuations may lead to fat bloom - tiny white spots appearing on the surface due to cocoa butter separation. Step 3: Taste Your Chocolate Mindfully Before tasting, allow the chocolate to come slightly closer to room temperature. Taking small bites enables better appreciation of the aromas and flavors. Chew slowly to release the compounds responsible for taste onto your tongue. Key Tip: Engage all senses while eating chocolate - sight, smell, touch, sound (the snap when breaking a piece), and finally taste. Step 4: Savor the Aromas Take a moment to breathe in deeply near the chocolate before consuming. The scent plays an essential role in our perception of taste. High-quality chocolate often carries nuanced fragrances ranging from fruity and floral notes to earthy undertones. Step 5: Identify Flavors As you let the chocolate melt on your tongue, focus on identifying individual flavors. With practice, you might notice hints of coffee, dried fruits, nuts, tobacco, spices, etc., especially in darker chocolates. Step 6: Pair Your Chocolate To enhance the overall experience, try pairing your chocolate with complementary beverages or foods. For example, red wine pairs well with dark chocolate, while tea works nicely with milder varieties. Nuts, berries, bread, cheese, and even champagne can make great companions too! Key Tip: Experiment with different combinations to discover what suits your palate best. Conclusion: Taking the time to properly enjoy chocolate turns a simple indulgence into a pleasurable sensory journey. Remember, there are no strict rules here; everyone's preferences vary. So feel free to adapt these guidelines according to your liking. Happy tasting! And he goes no and he goes... Clocks use different ways to measure time. Clocks need some sort of steady beat or motion to track the change in time. Ancient water clocks worked by the steady movement of water from a container with a hole in the bottom to another container without a hole. Other clocks use pendulums, springs and gears to track the change in time correctly. They only rhyme they're not like real jokes. That's like a new world meal, isn't it? The "second law" says how much a force changes how a thing moves. When there is a net outside force on an object, its velocity (speed and direction of motion) will change. How fast the velocity changes is called the acceleration. Newton's second law says that bigger forces produce more acceleration. But objects with a lot of stuff in them (mass) are harder to push, so they do not accelerate as much. Another way of saying this is that the net force on an object equals the rate of change of its momentum. The momentum measures how much mass is in a thing, how fast it is going, and which direction it is going. So forces change the momentum, but how much they can change the speed and direction of motion still depends on mass. He puts everything to his mouth but... You want some bread and butter Chi? Cole played piano in New York City and Los Angeles, California when he was a young man. In 1937, he formed a group that played jazz music. Rationale: Phoneme awareness is essential to begin the reading process. Therefore, to be successful readers, children need to be able to recognize phonemes in spoken words and the letters they represent. In this lesson, students will learn the correspondence g = /g/ in spoken and written words. They will practice using and identifying the letter g. Giggle, Giggle, Quack By: Doreen Cronin Poster with the following: Tongue Twister- Good girls get the giggles and give them to guys. Upper- and lowercase drawings of G Pictures of things: guitar, grape, gum, glue, fish, bear, duck, cat "G" word wall: bulletin board with upper and lowercase cutouts of the letter and room to put things around Worksheet with pictures of words with the /g/ sound and some that do not have it: dog, cat, flag, girl, frog, boat, house, tree, pig, car, grape, guitar 1. Explain that every letter makes a sound that we can make with our mouth. "Today we are going to learn about the letter g. It makes the /g/ sound. The /g/ sound can either be at the beginning, middle, or end of a word. With practice today, you will be able to pick out the letter g and the /g/ sound in any word!" 2. Relate the /g/ sound to the students. "Now turn to your friend and give them a gift with your arms stretched out. Can you hear the /g/ sound in both of those words? When you hear the /g/ sound today, put your arms out and give your friend a gift." 3. Give the students a tongue twister to help them remember the sound. "Now we are going to try a tongue twister to help us remember the /g/ sound. I will say it first and then you can repeat it. Good girls get the giggles and give them to guys. (Let them repeat the saying) Now when we say it together, I want you to stretch out the /g/ sound and give your friend a gift when you hear it. GGGoog gggirls ggget the gggiggggggles and gggive them to ggguys. 4. Point out the letter g on the poster and tell students to get their pencil and paper ready. "We can write the /g/ sound if we use the letter g. First, I am going to show you how to write the lowercase g and then we will move on to the uppercase one. Take your pencil and make an a, then go down to the ditch and make a basket. If the ball drops it goes in the basket. So make your ball and drop it into the basket. Write ten more just like that one. Now we will practice the uppercase g. First, draw an uppercase C, then come up to the fence and give him a tray to hold straight. So uppercase C, up and give him a tray. Great, now practice writing ten more of those." 5. See if the students can hear the /g/ in spoken words. "Now I'm going to see if you can hear the /g/ sound in some words. The sound may be at the beginning or end of words. When you hear the /g/ sound, reach out and give your friend a gift. Do you hear the /g/ sound in gum or boy? Dog or cat? Hat or get? Log or pot? Grip or drip? Flag or top? Sack or frog? Shoes or pig?" 6. Now see if students can recognize the letter g in words with pictures of the words. "Now I'm going to show you some pictures of some things that begin with the letter g. Raise your hand if you think the word starts with the letter and tell me why you think that. Show pictures: guitar, bear, grape, duck, gum, fish, glue, and cat. When the students have chosen the correct pictures, put the pictures on a "G" word wall and display in the classroom. 7. Read Giggle, Giggle, Quack to the students. This story is about a duck who always gets in trouble when the farmer leaves. When the farmer is away on a weekend, the duck leaves notes that the caretaker thinks is from the farmer himself. The duck asks for food, baths, and movies, but he takes it too far! Will Farmer Brown get back in time to stop the duck? You'll have to read to find out! Have the students give a gift to their friend whenever they hear the /g/ sound. After reading, ask the children which words they heard with the /g/ sound and make an index card with the word on it to put on the "G" word wall. 8. For assessment: Give students the worksheet with pictures and have them color the words that have the /g/ sound in them. Display on word wall. Beginning Reading Design Rationale: In order to become better readers and decoders, children need to understand that letters represent vocal gestures or phonemes. In order to develop an understanding of words and letters, children need to learn correspondences. This lesson will help students identify the correspondence e=/e/ (short e). The lesson will develop the student's awareness of e=/e/, by giving them instruction and practice on how to form the short e sound, as well as practice reading decodable text containing the short e sound. The students will receive instruction in the decoding of short e words, as well as practicing spelling the words themselves. Materials: Chalkboard, chalk, chart with tongue twister written on it (Eddy the elephant was envious of the elk's eggs), primary paper, pencils, a copy of Red Gets Fed for each student, flash cards with letterbox words written on them (bend, pest, spend, went, end), Elkonin boxes for every student, letter manipulatives for every student (e, n, d, b, w, t, p, s) overhead Elkonin boxes, and overhead letter manipulatives (e, n, d, b, w, t, p, s), overhead projector, single card with the letter e on it. Procedure: 1. Introduce the e=/e/ correspondence and how to spot it in written text. Say: Today we are going to work with the letter e in written text (hold the letter e card for the students to see) The letter e makes the /e/ sound. 2. How does our mouth move when we say /e/? Our mouth is open with our tongue behind our bottom teeth. This is the sound an old man might make when he didn't hear what someone said. (model cupping your hand behind your ear while stretching out the /e/ sound.) Now you pretend like you are trying to hear what someone said while saying the sound /e/." 3. Let me show you how to find the /e/ in the word bed. I'm going to say it in slow motion and listen for the old man hearing sound Bb-e-e-e-d. Slower: Bb-e-e-e-e-d. Did you hear it? I heard the old man. 4. Now let's try a tongue twister [use the chart]. I am going to read it once and then I want all of us to read it together. "Eddy the elephant was envious of the elk's eggs." Good. Now this time when we say it I want everyone to do their "old man hearing" motion while we drag out all of those e's. EEEEEddy the eeeelephant was eeeenvious of the eeeeelk's eeeeggs. Good job! 5. Call on the students to tell you how they know the answer: Do you hear /e/ in best or math? Blend or bad? Good or ever? Spend or black? Say: Let's see if you can spot the mouth move /e/ in some words. Cup your hand behind your ear if you hear /e/: The excited elf made toy elephants for the children. 6. Now use the Elkonin boxes to do a class letterbox lesson. Hand out the letterbox sheets and the letters to each student and have them put the letters on the lower case side. The letters that they will need are: e, n, b, d, w, t, p, s. Put your letters and letterboxes on the overhead and model how to do a word. Remind them that each letterbox stands for each individual sound not each individual letter. Say: Now I am going to show you an example of what we are going to do. Model for student's how the LBL works. Say: Now, let's do the word "end." She wanted to end the game. Let's see I hear /e/ first, what letter makes the sound /e/? So e goes in the first letter box. Then I hear the /n/ sound so the letter n goes in my second letterbox and then I hear /d/ what letter makes that sound? "d" good job! Now everyone let's see if you all can do a couple of words. Words: 3-(let, bed), 4-(tend, shred, help, dress,), 5-(blend, slept, stress). Read each word with a sentence that contains the word you are working on. Walk around the classroom and make sure each student is on task. Give them a couple of minutes to figure out their letters and time to correct themselves. If a student has misspelled a word, repeat it to them just as hey have spelled it and see if they can figure out what is wrong. If they don't correct then give them the word. After each student is finished model the correct spelling on the overhead and move onto the next word. a.[use flashcards with each of the letterbox words written on them and have the students read the word out to you] Tell me what this word says. Good job! You all are doing a great job recognizing the old man hearing /e/ sound. b.[introduce the decodable book: Red Gets Fed] We are all going to read Red Gets Fed. Have you ever had a pet that wanted to be fed all of the time? Well in this book, Red the dog begs everyone in his family for food. Let's read to see if he gets fed. c.Have each student write a message about their favorite pet while you call each student to your desk one at a time to identify e's in spoken words. Assessment: To assess each student, I would have them come up individually and read three pages of Red Gets Fed. I will also test their ability to recognize the /e/ sound. While they are writing their message, I will have them come up to my desk and identify e's in spoken words so that way I can make sure they have a good understanding of the phoneme and grapheme. Murray, B.A., and Lesniak, T. (1999) The Letterbox Lesson: A hands on approach for teaching decoding. The Red Gets Fed, Asbury, Sarah. http://www.auburn.edu/rdggenie/insp/asburybr.html. Many samples, particularly cells, can appear quite transparent under the microscope. The internal parts of the cells, the organelles, are so transparent that they are often difficult to see. Biologists have developed a number of stains that help them see the cells and their organelles by adding color to their transparent parts. While many biological stains are for advanced study only, there are some that are easy to obtain and use. Some readily available stains are: food coloring, iodine, malachite green (ick fish cure), and methylene blue. Food coloring can be found at a grocery store, and iodine can be found at a pharmacy. The last two stains, malachite green and methylene blue, can be purchased at aquarium shops. Interestingly, certain stains color certain parts of a cell. Scientists choose specific stains when they want to look at a particular part of a cell. You can experiment with the stains listed above to see which parts of the cell each one colors. Make sure you have an adult to supervise before you use stains. Stains not only add color to cells, but also hands, clothes, and nearly everything else. Use with caution and prepare your work area first with layers of newspaper. Stain cells and compare to unstained cells. Tools & Materials - 2 flat slides - 2 cover slips - cheek cells - stain (food coloring, iodine, malachite green or methylene blue) - paper towel Before you begin, decide which stain you will use for the experiment. Cells from the inside lining of your cheek are good for learning how to stain. Gently scrape the inside of your mouth with the flat side of a toothpick. This scraping will collect some of your cheek cells. (Don't worry, these cells are constantly being shed from your mouth so they will not be missed!) Place the cells on a flat slide and make a wet mount as you did in the "Wet mounts" activity. Now repeat this procedure so that you have two wet mounts of cells from the inside of your cheek. Now you need to add stain to one slide. To add stain, put a drop of the stain next to the cover slip on the slide and then draw it under the cover slip by placing a piece of paper towel against the other side of the cover slip. The paper towel will soak up the water, drawing the stain under the cover slip around the cell. Drawing the stain under the cell is called "wicking." Look at the stained slide under the microscope. Draw what you see. Now look at the unstained slide under the microscope. Is it different? Draw what you see. Try using different stains and different types of cells, both plant and animal. Make drawings of what you see. Science Learning Network Home / SLN Inquiry Resources / 1996 Museum of Science, Boston Let's bring these wooden people to the beach. Uh don't eat the tag silly boy. They now have to score more runs than the other team managed to score. If they score more runs before ten wickets are taken, they win. If they do not, the other team wins. Folding the folder Make the size that is right for the number of pieces you have in your projects Basic Lapbook Base - Open a file folder and lay it flat. - Fold both right and left edges toward the center so they meet and close like a pair of shutters. - Crease firmly. Base with Single or Double Extensions - Complete the basic lapbook base. - Open base and lay flat. - Cut another folder in half or use a sheet of cardstock for the extension. - Lay the extension in the center of folder at either the top or bottom. (You may add two extensions if need be; one at the top and one at the bottom). - Attach to base with clear packing tape. Double Folder Base - Make two base folders. - Open them and lay them side by side with outer flaps pointing straight up, not flat. - Where the two flaps meet glue them together. - Fold center flap to one side, fold both shutters in and close folders like a book. - Use for all subjects, just add English and Math at the student's grade level - Add onto a complete boxed curriculum - Use for a fun break or child-led learning = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV7/LW/kevlw7.cha = = = You want to see the baby and stuff? Title: How to Change Camera Angles in NBA 2K21 Introduction: Changing camera angles in NBA 2K21 can greatly enhance your gaming experience by providing you with different perspectives of the game. This tutorial will guide you through the process of changing camera angles in NBA 2K21, including detailed explanations for each step and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Access the Game Settings Menu To change the camera angle in NBA 2K21, first access the game settings menu. To do this, press the 'Options' or 'Start' button on your controller while you are at the main screen of the game. This will bring up the main game options menu. Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with the layout of the game settings menu so that you can quickly navigate to the camera settings when needed. Step 2: Navigate to the Camera Settings From the main game options menu, scroll down until you see the 'Settings' option. Select this option using your controller's directional pad or left joystick. Once you have entered the settings menu, scroll down again until you find the 'Camera' sub-menu. Guideline: The location of various menus and options may differ slightly depending on which platform (PlayStation, Xbox, PC) you are playing NBA 2K21 on. However, the overall navigation structure should be similar across all platforms. Step 3: Choose Your Preferred Camera Angle Once you have accessed the Camera settings menu, you will see several preset camera angles to choose from. These include: * Broadcast: Provides an over-the-shoulder view similar to what you would see during a televised basketball game. * 2K Cam: Offers a dynamic, close-up perspective that follows the action closely. * Courtside: Gives you a courtside seat view of the entire court. * ISO Cam: A unique angled view designed to mimic popular sports broadcasting styles. Select the camera angle that best suits your preferences and gaming style. Key Tips: * Experiment with different camera angles to determine which one provides the most comfortable viewing experience for you. * Keep in mind that some camera angles may make it more difficult to accurately judge player positioning and movements. Step 4: Adjust Additional Camera Options (Optional) If desired, you can further customize your camera settings by adjusting additional options within the Camera settings menu. Some examples include: * Zoom Level: Allows you to control how far or close the camera is positioned relative to the players. * Height: Lets you set the vertical height of the camera. * Angle: Enables you to fine-tune the horizontal tilt of the camera. * Swivel Speed: Determines how quickly the camera pans to keep up with the action. Adjust these settings as necessary to create your ideal camera setup. Step 5: Save Your Custom Camera Settings After making any changes to your camera settings, remember to save them before exiting the settings menu. Look for the 'Save Changes' or 'Apply' button usually located near the bottom of the screen. By saving your preferred camera settings, you ensure that they will remain consistent whenever you play NBA 2K21. Conclusion: By following the steps outlined above, you now know how to effectively change camera angles in NBA 2K21. Remember that experimentation and personal preference are crucial factors when determining the optimal camera setting for your individual needs. With practice and patience, you'll soon discover the perfect camera angle that enhances both your enjoyment and success in NBA 2K21. Find the Author Of a Website: A Comprehensive Guide ============================================== Introduction ------------ In today's digital age, finding the author of a website is an essential skill that can help you verify information, establish credibility, or simply satisfy your curiosity. This comprehensive guide will walk you through various methods to find the author of a website using different techniques and tools available online. The steps provided here are designed with beginners in mind but also contain valuable insights for experienced researchers. By following these procedures meticulously, you will enhance your detective skills while gaining knowledge about web technologies and privacy concerns. ### Why Should You Find the Author Of a Website? ---------------------------------------------- 1. **Verifying Information**: Knowing who created the content allows you to assess its reliability and accuracy more effectively. It's crucial when dealing with sensitive subjects like health advice, financial guidance, or legal matters. 2. **Establishing Credibility**: Recognizing the expertise behind compelling arguments strengthens trust in their validity. For instance, if you come across groundbreaking research findings, acknowledging the authors adds weight to the discovery. 3. **Contacting Experts**: When exploring niche topics, discovering professionals passionate about similar interests opens opportunities for collaboration, networking, or seeking expert opinions. 4. **Understanding Ownership**: Identifying ownership patterns provides insights into business structures, marketing strategies, and industry trends. Understanding these aspects may inspire improvements in your projects or ventures. ### Key Tips and Guidelines -------------------------- * Be patient: Locating the elusive figure behind a website might require persistence since some authors prioritize privacy over public exposure. * Use multiple sources: Combine several search methods to increase chances of success. One approach may yield partial results, whereas another could fill gaps in your investigation. * Stay ethical: Respect copyright laws and avoid misusing personal data obtained during your quest. Always give credit where due by citing sources appropriately. Method 1: Analyzing Webpage Source Code --------------------------------------- Most websites consist of HTML (HyperText Markup Language), CSS (Cascading Style Sheets), JavaScript, and other files that render visual elements. These components reside within the source code accessible via any browser's developer console. Here's how to navigate this hidden world: ### Step 1: Access Developer Console Launch your preferred browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, etc.) and visit the target website. Right-click anywhere on the page and select "Inspect" or press `F12` to open the developer console. ![Developer Console]( Build a village for king and queen. Its muscle pushes food down into the stomach. The oesophagus can contract or expand to allow for the passage of food. The muscular movement that pushes the food down the oesophagus is called peristalsis. At the entrance to the stomach there is a ring of muscle called a sphincter. This is usually closed, but relaxes as food approaches, allowing it to enter the stomach. In the stomach, the food is churned until it turns into a soupy mixture called chyme. The band was originally named Earth and they played blues-rock music. In the band's early days Tony Iommi had a work accident that cut off the tips of his middle and ring finger on his right hand. He began tuning his guitar lower to slacken his strings to make playing easier on his wounded fingers. The result was a darker, deeper tone to his guitar sound which the band felt made their music sound dark and scary. The music also featured unusual key changes and time signatures (metre). They released their first album "Black Sabbath" in 1970. Their most successful single, titled "Paranoid", produced by Rodger Bain, came off their second album of the same name, also released in 1970. By comparing prices, features, customer reviews, and seller ratings, you can find the best product that fits your needs and budget. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to compare products on Amazon: 1. Visit <https://www.amazon.com> and sign in to your account (if necessary). Having an Amazon account allows you to access wish lists, order history, and other personalized features. If you don't have an account, consider creating one for a more seamless shopping experience. 2. Use the search bar at the top of the page to look up the type of product you want to buy. For example, if you're looking for headphones, enter 'headphones' into the search bar and hit Enter or click the magnifying glass icon. 3. Browse through the search results to identify potential products to compare. You may filter the results by price range, brand, customer rating, prime eligibility, and other criteria using the options on the left sidebar. This initial browsing will give you an idea about which products are popular and well-reviewed within your preferred specifications. 4. Click on the first product title to view its detail page. Familiarize yourself with its main features, technical specifications, pricing, and available colors or styles. Read the product description carefully to ensure it meets your expectations and requirements. Look for any unique selling points or distinguishing factors compared to similar items. 5. Scroll down to the Customer Reviews section below the product information. Pay attention to both the overall star rating and individual review scores from verified customers. Reading through several positive and negative reviews can provide insights into the pros and cons of the product. Focus on reviews written by users who share similar preferences or concerns as yours. 6. Check the Questions & Answers section under Customer Reviews. This area contains questions asked by shoppers and answered either by product experts, manufacturers, or fellow customers. It is helpful to see what additional information others might be seeking regarding the product and whether those queries align with your own interests. 7. Return to the search results page and select another contending item to compare against the first product. Repeat steps 4-6 for this second product, taking notes on their differences and similarities in terms of quality, performance, design, durability, and value. 8. Consider visiting third-party websites such as CNET, PCMag, or Consumer Reports to read expert opinions and comparisons related to your chosen products. These resources often offer unbiased evaluations based on rigorous testing methodologies, providing valuable context alongside user experiences. 9. After gathering enough data, weigh the advantages and disadvantages of each product. Determine which aspects matter most to you - price, functionality, aesthetics, etc., then rank them accordingly. Based on these priorities, choose the product that offers the best balance between cost and benefits. 10. Before finalizing your decision, double-check the availability of each product on Amazon. Verify the shipping timeframes, return policies, and seller ratings to avoid potential complications during checkout and post-purchase stages. Remember to utilize Prime membership perks like free two-day shipping whenever possible. 11. Once satisfied with your comparison findings, add your selected product to the cart and proceed to checkout. Confirm your delivery address, payment details, and review all charges before placing the order. By following this comprehensive guide, you should feel confident making educated purchasing choices while navigating Amazon's vast marketplace. Happy shopping! This is a hormone produced by the body when viruses are present. In the 18th century, Pierre-Louis Moreau de Maupertuis made experiments with scorpion venom and observed that certain dogs and mice were immune to this venom. This and other observations of acquired immunity led to Louis Pasteur (1822-1895) developing vaccination and the germ theory of disease. Pasteur's theory was in direct opposition to contemporary theories of disease, such as the miasma theory. It was not until the proofs Robert Koch (1843-1910) published in 1891 (for which he was awarded a Nobel Prize in 1905) that microorganisms were confirmed as the cause of infectious disease. Viruses were confirmed as human pathogens in 1901, when the yellow fever virus was discovered by Walter Reed (1851-1902). ooh, you like these what are these? Growing Independence and Fluency In order for students to read more fluently they need to be able to be more expressive, fast and smooth while they When children are able to decode words effortlessly reading becomes enjoyable for them, hopefully increasing the time they spend reading. able to have more fun reading. To improve fluency you need to take a approach with lots of repetitive practice. This lesson aims at helping read smoother, faster and more expressively! Stopwatch for every two children, a copy of "Bud the Sub" with marks every ten words for every student ("Charlie" for the advanced readers), a copy of the race track for every student (click here), a copy of the four race cars page for every student (click here), crayons, scissors, markers for students to color their cars, glue, "road" cover up sticks for every two students, and a sentence strip with "Gus and Meg fell off the log." 1.) I will introduce the lesson by briefly explaining the importance of reading with speed and fluency: Today we are going to work on making our reading sound a little better. I am going to read part of this book two ways and I want you to tell me if I do a better job of reading the first or second way. Listen to the first way I read. (I will read the sentence strip the way a non-fluent reader would). "G-u-s a-n-d M-e-g f-e-ll o-ff th-e l-o-g" That isn't very fun to listen to is it? Let's see if the second way I can read sounds any better. (I will read the same sentence with fluency and expression). "Gus and Meg fell off the log." Which way did you like better? The second way! Me, too! It takes a lot of practice to learn how to read like that. One of the best ways to work on this is to get reading faster and faster. Then, it is easy to add excitement to reading because it isn't hard to read the words anymore! Today, we are going to practice reading faster and faster. 2) The students will have already read In the Bud the Sub (lower readers) or Charlie (more advanced readers) with their reading groups depending on their reading level. I will pair the students off homogenously so that they will be able to use the same book. 3) Pass out the stop watches, various books, race tracks, and cars to each group. While one partner is the reader, the other will be the timer. The reader will be timed for one minute. If you are the reader, I want you to read as many words as you can. If you come to a word that you don't know use your "road" cover up stick to try to figure it out. Review cover-up strategy. Remember that when we come to a word we are having trouble with you can use your cover-up stick by covering the word then as you read you reveal one letter at a time to help you sound it out. Then blend it all together. Let's try it with the sentence I read earlier. Model for students with the sentence strip. Then let the whole class practice as you cover up parts of words. If you still are having trouble, ask your partner for help. I will also be walking around to help you if you need it. The partners will trade off several times so that they will get faster and faster. Before they start, pass out the race tracks and cars. When the timer has timed for one minute, she or he will record the number of words that the reader has read and place the car on that number on the race track. The books have marks every ten words to make it easier for you to count. All, you have to do is count by tens and then add on the extra words. If your partner reads 36 words in a minute you will glue a car between the 30 and the 40. Each of y'all will read the book four times, then you will switch jobs with your partner. I bet that your car will get further down the race track each time you read! Cars can be glued on to the track, or if you would like to reuse the race track make sure the students write the number of words down on the front of the car and the reader name and book on the back, and just have the students place their cars on the track. assess each student by looking at his or her progress on their race see if they were able to improve. The cars will be numbered, so I will many pages the student read each of the four times. Before I take up tracks, I will make sure that their name, date, and the title of the book is on the top of the page. This way I can reference back and have documentation of their progress during the year. Asbury, Sarah. Title: How to Cook Perfect Hard Boiled Eggs with Yellow Yolks and Firm But Tender Whites Introduction: Hard boiling eggs seems like a simple task, but achieving that perfect yellow yolk without any green tinge and firm yet tender whites can be tricky. This tutorial will guide you through the process, providing detailed steps, helpful tips, and scientific explanations so you can cook hard-boiled eggs like a pro every time. Key Tips before Starting: * Use older eggs if possible; fresh eggs are more difficult to peel. * Gently place eggs into the pot to avoid cracking them. * Don't overcrowd the pot; allow room for even heat distribution around each egg. Step 1: Prepare Your Equipment Gather the following equipment: * A large saucepan or pot * Enough water to cover the eggs by at least an inch * Slotted spoon or tongs * Bowl filled with ice water Step 2: Place Eggs in Saucepan Place your cold (not straight from the refrigerator) eggs gently into the bottom of the saucepan. Avoid stacking eggs as this may lead to uneven cooking or cracking during the boiling process. Step 3: Fill Saucepan with Cold Water Pour enough cold tap water into the pan until the eggs are covered by about one inch. Make sure all eggs are submerged underwater. Step 4: Slowly Bring Water to a Boil Place the saucepan on medium-high heat and slowly bring the water to a rolling boil. The slow heating prevents sudden temperature changes which could cause the eggs to crack. It also ensures gradual expansion inside the shell, reducing risk of cracks. Step 5: Turn Off Heat Once Rolling Boil is Reached Once the water reaches a full boil, turn off the heat immediately. Keep the pan undisturbed on the hot burner. Leaving the eggs in the hot water allows residual heat to continue cooking them gently. Step 6: Let Eggs Sit in Hot Water Allow the eggs to sit in the hot water for approximately 9-12 minutes depending on their size. Jumbo eggs might require closer to 12 minutes while small ones could need only 9 minutes. During this period, the proteins within the egg coagulate, forming a solid mass. Step 7: Cool Down Immediately After Cooking Time After the specified duration, carefully drain the hot water from the pan using a slotted spoon or tongs. Transfer the eggs directly into the bowl of ice water. Rapid cooling stops further cooking and makes the eggs easier to handle and peel. Step 8: Peeling the Eggs Tap the egg gently on a hard surface to create several small cracks around its circumference. Start peeling at the wide end where there's an air pocket between the shell and white. Under running cool water, slide your thumb under the membrane to loosen it, then pull the rest off. Conclusion: By following these steps meticulously, you'll consistently produce hard-boiled eggs with bright yellow yolks encased in firm but tender whites - no more unappetizing green rings! Remember, patience and gentle handling throughout the process guarantees success. Enjoy your perfectly cooked hard-boiled eggs! Trying to figure out what they want to say. Title: How to Buy High Heels Buying high heels can be an exciting yet daunting task due to the wide variety of styles, brands, and fits available in the market. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of buying high heels that are both stylish and comfortable, ensuring you make a well-informed decision. ### Step 1: Determine Your Purpose Before purchasing high heels, determine your purpose. Are they for work, parties, or everyday wear? Knowing this will help narrow down your options and ensure you find the right pair for the occasion. For instance, if they're for work, consider block heels or wedges for added comfort during prolonged standing or walking. ### Step 2: Measure Your Foot Size Accurately measure your foot size before making any purchase. Ill-fitting shoes can lead to discomfort and potential health issues like bunions or corns. To get accurate measurements, stand with your back against a wall, place a piece of paper under your feet, and mark where your longest toe ends and heel begins. Measure the distance between these two points in inches or centimeters. Most shoe stores offer free fittings; take advantage of this service if possible. Key Tip: Remember that different brands may have slightly varying sizes. When shopping online, check customer reviews regarding sizing as some manufacturers tend to run small or large. ### Step 3: Understand Different Heel Types There are various types of high heels, each having its unique characteristics and suitability for different occasions: 1. Stilettos: These are thin, tall heels (over 4 inches) offering maximum height but less stability. They're ideal for formal events or photo shoots where there is minimal movement involved. The shields might be useful against a Bushman's arrows or against an assagy, but were useless against a bullet. We each carried a bag of Indian corn, and several boys who accompanied us also carried bags of corn. I afterwards found out that if Inyati succeeded in driving off the Bushmen, he was to receive a reward of cattle from the people he had assisted, and they were to be bound to help him in any expedition that he undertook against his enemies. Title: How to Clean a Birdbath A clean birdbath is essential for attracting birds to your garden and ensuring their health. Dirty birdbaths can become breeding grounds for bacteria and diseases that may harm birds. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of cleaning a birdbath, providing in-depth explanations for each step along with helpful tips and guidelines. By following these steps, you'll create an inviting and safe space for birds to bathe and drink water. **Step 1: Empty the Birdbath** Begin by removing any standing water from the birdbath. Use a bucket or a container to transfer the water into a nearby plant or drainage area. Removing stagnant water reduces the risk of bacterial growth and mosquito larvae development. **Step 2: Scrub the Interior Surface** Use a stiff-bristled brush to scrub the interior surface of the birdbath thoroughly. Pay close attention to corners, crevices, and textured surfaces where dirt and algae might accumulate. A long-handled brush makes this task easier, especially if your birdbath has a deep basin. Regularly cleaning the birdbath inhibits the buildup of harmful substances like algae and bacteria. **Step 3: Rinse the Birdbath** Rinelyour birdbath using a hose with a high-pressure nozzle or a power washer. Make sure all soap residue and loose debris are removed during rinsing. Thoroughly rinsed surfaces prevent chemical exposure to birds and promote faster drying. **Key Tips:** * Avoid using harsh chemicals when cleaning a birdbath as they could be toxic to birds. Stick to mild dish soap or vinegar solutions instead. * If possible, wear gloves while handling dirty water and scrubbing the birdbath to maintain personal hygiene. **Step 4: Soak the Birdbath (Optional)** If your birdbath has stubborn grime or algae buildups, consider soaking it in a diluted bleach solution (one part bleach to nine parts water) for about ten minutes. Alternatively, use undiluted white vinegar for up to twenty minutes. These solutions help break down tough deposits without causing damage to most materials used in making birdbaths. Remember always to rinse the birdbath thoroughly after soaking. **Step 5: Dry the Birdbath** Allow the birdbath to air dry completely before refilling it with fresh water. Sunlight naturally disinfects the birdbath during the drying process. Additionally, evaporating moisture deters the return of mold and mildew. **Guidelines:** * It's best to clean your birdbath at least once a week during warmer months when usage increases. During colder periods, monthly cleanings should suffice unless the climate is particularly humid. * Consider placing your birdbath near trees or bushes to provide shelter and resting spots for birds visiting your garden oasis. However, avoid overhanging branches directly above the bath, which could drip sap or contaminate the water. By regularly maintaining a clean birdbath according to these instructions, you're creating a healthy environment for backyard birds to enjoy. They'll appreciate the effort, and you'll reap the rewards of observing beautiful wildlife right outside your window! Mommy are you having fun on the boat? Um do you think there's any toy that... Title: How to Make and Drink Coconut Water Kefir Coconut water kefir is a probiotic-rich beverage with numerous health benefits. It's made by fermenting coconut water with kefir grains, which are cultures of bacteria and yeast. This tutorial will guide you through the process of making and drinking coconut water kefir at home, ensuring clarity and practicality every step of the way. **Step 1:** Gather Your Ingredients and Equipment To make coconut water kefir, gather these ingredients and equipment: * **Ingredients:** + Fresh young Thai coconuts (4-6) or store-bought pure coconut water (2 quarts / 1.9 liters) + Kefir grains (1-2 tablespoons) - available online or from a friend who makes kefir * **Equipment:** + Blender (for fresh coconut water) D sseldorf has an opera, more than 30 cinemas, a well-known theatre, and a rich cultural scene. [GAR ignores the ball; she's looking at the other ball she is holding] Not all humans have the same number of copies of the AMY1 gene. Populations which rely on carbohydrates have a higher number of AMY1 copies than populations that eat little starch. The number of AMY1 gene copies in humans can range from six copies in agricultural groups such as European-American and Japanese (two high starch populations) to only 2-3 copies in hunter-gatherer societies such as the Biaka, Datog, and Yakuts. [as Hal attempts to place marker in box] Title: How to Execute a Successful Slip in Soccer Slipping in soccer is a tactical move used by players to get past defenders and create scoring opportunities. When executed correctly, slips can lead to breakaways, through balls, and goals. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on executing a successful slip, including key tips and guidelines. **Step 1: Understand the Concept of a Slip** A slip in soccer involves using quick movements and deception to trick a defender into thinking you are going one way, only to quickly change direction and go the other way. The goal is to gain separation from the defender and create space for yourself or a teammate. **Step 2: Identify the Right Moment to Attempt a Slip** Timing is crucial when attempting a slip. Look for moments when the defender is slightly out of position or distracted. This could be during a transition period after your team wins possession or when the defender is focusing on another player. Additionally, pay attention to the defender's body language and footwork; if they appear unbalanced or hesitant, this may indicate an opportunity to execute a slip. **Step 3: Position Your Body Correctly** To successfully perform a slip, you must first properly position your body. Start by facing the defender with your shoulders squared off. This will make it more difficult for them to predict which direction you will move. Keep your knees bent and maintain a low center of gravity, enabling you to quickly change directions. **Step 4: Use Quick Feints and Fakes** Use small, rapid movements to confuse the defender about your intended direction. For example, try taking a step in one direction before rapidly shifting your weight and moving in the opposite direction. These feints should be subtle but deliberate, making it challenging for the defender to anticipate your next move. **Step 5: Explosively Change Directions** Once you have successfully misdirected the defender, explosively change directions and accelerate away from them. Focus on maintaining good balance and proper running form as you change direction. A powerful first step is essential for gaining separation from the defender. **Step 6: Create Space and Distribute the Ball (If Necessary)** After successfully executing the slip, focus on creating space between yourself and the defender. If you have a clear path towards the goal, continue dribbling forward. However, if a teammate is in a better position to score, look for opportunities to distribute the ball via a through pass or cross. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Practice regularly: Mastering the slip requires consistent practice and repetition. Set aside time to work on your skills, either alone or with a training partner. * Stay focused: Maintain concentration throughout the entire process, from identifying the right moment to distributing the ball. Lapses in focus can result in missed opportunities or turnovers. * Remain patient: Don't rush into performing a slip without carefully considering the situation. Patience is vital for recognizing the optimal moment to attempt a slip. * Develop good peripheral vision: Being able to perceive what is happening around you allows for better decision-making during high-pressure situations. Regularly train your peripheral vision to improve your ability to read the field effectively. * Study professional players: Watch videos of skilled soccer players executing slips and analyze their techniques. Learning from experienced professionals can help refine your own approach to slipping. [pours a glass of milk with a taste of coffee for Sarah] They're to put on the board and make words. Title: How to Browse the Internet Using the Terminal in Linux Introduction: While graphical web browsers like Firefox and Chrome are popular choices for internet browsing, Linux also offers command-line based options that can be used within the terminal. This approach might seem daunting at first, but it has its advantages, such as speed, simplicity, and lower resource usage. Here's our comprehensive guide on navigating the World Wide Web through your Linux terminal. Step 1: Install a Command Line Browser There are several command line browsers available, including 'Lynx', 'w3m', 'Elinks', and 'Links'. We will demonstrate using 'Lynx' due to its wide popularity and ease of installation. To install Lynx, open your terminal (usually Ctrl+Alt+T) and type: `sudo apt install lynx` (For Debian-based distributions like Ubuntu) or `sudo yum install lynx` (For Red Hat-based distributions like CentOS or Fedora) This command prompts superuser access to download and install the package from official repositories. Step 2: Basic Navigation Commands Launch Lynx by simply typing 'lynx' followed by a space and then the URL you wish to visit. For example: `lynx https://www.example.com` Use arrow keys to scroll up and down pages. Press 'Enter' to follow links. Type 'g' followed by a number to jump directly to that page section if headers are displayed numerically (often denoted with an equals sign '='). Key Tips & Guidelines: * Use Tab key to switch between clickable elements (like buttons or input fields). * Hit Enter to activate these elements. * If presented with multiple results after searching something, type '/' and then what you want to search for within those results. Step 3: Accessing Previous Pages To go back one page, press the left bracket '['. Conversely, pressing the right bracket ']' takes you forward again. You can also hit 'b' to view the history and choose which page to return to. Step 4: Bookmarking Sites You can bookmark sites for quicker navigation later. While viewing a site, press 'o' and enter the filename when prompted. Later, load this file with 'g' while inside the 'Lynx Bookmarks' menu accessed via 'b'. Step 5: Downloading Files When you encounter a link leading to a file download, hitting 'd' instead of 'Enter' adds it to a download queue managed by lynx. After finishing browsing, exit Lynx and all queued files will start downloading automatically into the current working directory. Alternatively, manually initiate downloads using 'wget <URL>' or 'curl -O <URL>'. Step 6: Copy Text Press 'v' to toggle View mode where text becomes selectable. Now move cursor over the desired text, then press 'Space' to copy. Exit View mode using Esc. Copied text is stored in clipboard manager until cleared or overwritten. Conclusion: Though seemingly archaic compared to modern GUI browsers, mastering command line browsing opens new possibilities for efficient interaction with online content. From script automation to low-resource environments, knowing how to browse the internet using the terminal in Linux proves incredibly useful. [a little lower pitch; 13 sec silence interspersed with squawks and rings] Hello young scholars! Today, let's talk about a fun and exciting concept called "logical reasoning." Logical reasoning helps us understand whether statements are true or false based on certain conditions. Let's dive into an example together! Imagine you have a list of friends in your class, and you want to know if everyone who passed Physics (Phy) also passed Chemistry (Chem). Now, say you find out that there's a friend who excelled in both subjects - hooray! What does this tell us? Well, it means that there indeed is a student who passed Physics *and* another student who passed Chemistry. But here's the cool part - these could be the same person! So, yes, the statement holds true. Now, think about this: if you had a superstudent who aced all subjects, would it mean that every single classmate of theirs passed in those subjects too? Of course not! Just because one person did well doesn't guarantee others succeeded too. Let's twist our original statement a bit: if there's someone good at both Physics and Chemistry, then there should be individual students excelling in each subject separately. Does this work backward? Unfortunately, no. Even if there are distinct students doing well in either subject, it doesn't necessarily imply that one extraordinary learner mastered both. So remember, logical reasoning involves carefully considering the relationships between ideas and making sure to avoid assumptions when drawing conclusions. Keep practicing this skill, and soon enough, you'll become a pro at figuring things out! Happy learning! You see, I've got no landlord, Nolan--I'm in that position o' life that I can be independent." The people in Australia wanted to run their own country, and self-govern. The first governments in the colonies were run by Governors chosen by London. Soon the settlers wanted local government and more democracy. The New South Wales Legislative Council, was created in 1825 to advise the Governor of New South Wales, but it was not chosen by voters. William Wentworth established the Australian Patriotic Association (Australia's first political party) in 1835 to demand democratic government for New South Wales. In 1840, the Adelaide City Council and the Sydney City Council were started and some people could vote for them (but only men with a certain amount of money). Then, Australia's first parliamentary elections were held for the New South Wales Legislative Council in 1843, again with some limits on who could vote. "The Australian Colonies Government Act" [1850] allowed constitutions for New South Wales, Victoria, South Australia and Tasmania. In 1850 elections for legislative councils were also held in the colonies of Victoria, South Australia and Tasmania. Title: Have a Simple Hairstyle for School (Long Hair) Introduction: A simple hairstyle can be a lifesaver during those busy school mornings. This step-by-step guide will help you create a quick, easy, and stylish look that suits long hair. The following hairstyle is polished enough for school yet straightforward enough to pull off even when you're short on time. With some practice, this elegant half-updo will become second nature. **Materials Needed:** * A brush or comb * Two hair ties * Bobby pins (optional) * Hairspray (optional) Step 1: Brush Your Hair Brushing your hair before starting ensures there are no tangles or knots that could hinder the process. It also allows for a neater appearance overall. Use a detangling brush if necessary. Step 2: Section Off the Top Portion of Your Hair Create a section about two inches wide from temple to temple at the crown of your head. Gathering hair from the front enhances volume and frames your face nicely. Secure this top portion with a temporary hair tie. Key Tip: If your hair tends to fall flat quickly, backcomb the roots slightly under the tied-off section to add extra lift and body. Step 3: Divide the Ponytail into Two Equal Sections Remove the temporary hair tie carefully so as not to disturb the divided sections. Hold one half steady while preparing the other half for twisting. Guideline: Keep both parts equal to maintain balance and symmetry once twisted together. Step 4: Twist Each Section Individually Towards the Back Start by holding onto the end of one section and twist it clockwise around itself until it reaches close to the base of the ponytail. Repeat this process with the remaining section but counterclockwise. Step 5: Intertwine the Twists Together Bring the twisted sections together near the base of the ponytail. Cross the left twist over the right one and wrap them around each other, continuing downwards like a rope. Once they reach the ends, secure them with another hair tie. Step 6: Adjust and Fine-Tune (Optional) For any stray strands or loose pieces, incorporate them gently using bobby pins. You may also opt to lightly spritz your finished 'do with hairspray to ensure longevity throughout the day. Conclusion: This timeless half-updo should take no more than five minutes after initial practice sessions. Its simplicity makes it versatile - suitable for casual days, presentations, special events, or simply whenever you want to look put-together without spending too much time getting ready. Enjoy rocking your new go-to hairstyle! [perhaps a comment to MOT about Clyde] Who you going to write a letter to? Eating coconut oil can provide numerous health benefits due to its unique composition of medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs). This tutorial will guide you through the process of incorporating coconut oil into your diet, with detailed explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Choose High-Quality Coconut Oil** When purchasing coconut oil, look for unrefined, virgin, or extra-virgin varieties. These types are least processed and retain the most nutrients. Refined coconut oils may have fewer nutrients and potentially harmful additives. *Key Tip:* Opt for organic coconut oil whenever possible, as this ensures no pesticides were used during cultivation. **Step 2: Determine Your Daily Intake** The recommended daily intake varies depending on individual needs, but starting with one tablespoon per day is generally safe. Gradually increase the amount over time if desired. Some studies suggest that up to three tablespoons per day can offer additional health benefits without adverse effects. *Guideline:* Consult a healthcare professional before making significant changes to your diet, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions or take medications. **Step 3: Decide How to Consume It** There are several ways to consume coconut oil, including: a) Direct consumption - Simply eat a spoonful of coconut oil straight from the jar. However, be aware that consuming large amounts at once might cause digestive discomfort. b) Adding to hot beverages - Mix coconut oil into coffee or tea for an energy boost. The heat from these drinks makes it easier to blend the oil evenly. c) Cooking and baking - Replace other cooking fats like butter or vegetable oil with coconut oil when preparing meals or baked goods. Keep in mind that heating coconut oil above its smoke point (around 350 F/175 C) can degrade some beneficial compounds. d) Salad dressings - Combine coconut oil with vinegar, lemon juice, herbs, and spices to create flavorful salad dressings. e) Blending into smoothies - Add coconut oil to fruit or green smoothies for added nutrition and creaminess. *Tip:* Start by adding small quantities of coconut oil to food items until you become accustomed to the taste and texture. **Step 4: Monitor Any Changes in Digestion** Some people experience mild stomach upset, loose stools, or diarrhea after consuming large amounts of coconut oil. If this occurs, reduce your intake and gradually build up again over time. Drinking plenty of water and eating fiber-rich foods can also help alleviate any digestive issues. *Key Guideline:* Listen to your body's signals and adjust accordingly; everyone reacts differently to new dietary habits. **Step 5: Track Progress Towards Desired Outcomes** Keep track of any positive changes experienced after incorporating coconut oil into your diet, such as improved energy levels, weight loss, or better skin condition. Record serving sizes, frequency, and specific symptoms noticed. Sharing this information with a healthcare provider could prove valuable in assessing overall wellness. In conclusion, eating coconut oil has various potential health advantages, ranging from increased metabolism to enhanced cognitive function. By following these steps diligently and being attentive to individual responses, individuals can effectively incorporate this versatile ingredient into their everyday routine. Title: How to Hypnotize a Chicken Hypnotizing a chicken is a unique and interesting experience that has been practiced by farmers and poultry enthusiasts for many years. While it may seem like an unusual skill, understanding how to hypnotize a chicken can be useful in various situations such as trimming their nails or inspecting them for parasites. This guide will provide you with clear, concise, and easy-to-follow steps to successfully hypnotize a chicken. **Step 1:** Choose your chicken wisely Select a calm and cooperative chicken from your flock. A curious and friendly bird tends to respond better than shy or aggressive ones. Familiarize yourself with the chosen chicken so that it gets accustomed to your presence. Building trust between you and the chicken makes the process easier and more effective. **Why this step matters:** Selecting a suitable candidate reduces stress and anxiety for both the chicken and the handler. Choosing a tranquil environment also increases the chances of success. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** Spend time handling and interacting with chickens regularly to build rapport and make them comfortable around humans. Be patient during the selection process; don't force any particular chicken if they appear uncomfortable or anxious. --- **Step 2:** Find a flat surface Place the chicken on a smooth, level surface free of distractions. Ideally, find a spot away from other animals and loud noises. Make sure there are no obstacles nearby which could cause injury to the chicken while under hypnosis. **Why this step matters:** Creating a quiet and safe space allows the chicken to relax and focus solely on the person attempting to hypnotize them without fear of harm or disturbance. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** Remove any sharp objects near the working area, ensure proper lighting conditions, and avoid performing this technique during extreme temperatures (hot or cold) since these factors might affect the chicken's ability to enter into hypnosis. --- **Step 3:** Hold the chicken securely but gently Position the chicken onto its back using one hand to support its body weight against your torso. Place another hand firmly yet gently over the breast region to prevent the chicken from moving or struggling. Keep the head extended straight outward. ![Chicken position]( I like read reading books in the library. The Bhakti (Devotional) school takes its name from the Hindu term that signifies a blissful, selfless and overwhelming love of God as the beloved Father, Mother, Child, or whatever relationship finds appeal in the devotee's heart. The philosophy of Bhakti seeks to tap into the universal divinity through personal form, which explains the proliferation of so many gods and goddesses in India, often reflecting the singular inclinations of small regions or groups of people. Seen as a form of Yoga, or union, it seeks to dissolve the ego in God, since consciousness of the body and limited mind as self is seen to be a divisive factor in spiritual realization. Essentially, it is God who effects all change, who is the source of all works, who acts through the devotee as love and light. 'Sins' and evil-doings of the devotee are said to fall away of their own accord, the devotee shriven, limitedness even transcended, through the love of God. The Bhakti movements rejuvenated Hinduism through their intense expression of faith and their responsiveness to the emotional and philosophical needs of India. They can rightly be said to have affected the greatest wave of change in Hindu prayer and ritual since ancient times.
